ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: “Hɔgʋ dieke dɩ zɩna dembiŋ nan yigi nyuuti a mɩɩrɩ bʋadembiŋ aŋ ʋ saaŋ yi Emmanueli.”

ref: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
text: ta Josaya die mɩɩrɩ wa Jekonaya aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ, saŋŋa gie die ba yigi Izara vuosi a bɩrɩŋ ba yɔŋɩsɩ a yaa ba ga Babiloni tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

ref: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
text: Jakobu die mɩɩrɩ wa Josefu, vuodieke die dɩ yine Meri chʋrʋ, ta Meri dɩ mɩɩrɩna Yisa vuodieke ba wasɩnana Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩŋ Masia wa.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Azori die mɩɩrɩ wa Zadoki, ta Zadoki dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Achimi, ta Achimi dɩaŋ dɩ mɩɩrɩ Eludi.

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Die ba mɩɩrɩna Yisa Masia die wonde. Yisa nuŋ daa die wone Josefu ama die ba ye ka faarɩ tamba, ta Meri die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ sɩba dɩ ʋ yigi wo nyuuti, die Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka die vana ʋ yigi kanɩŋ nyuuti mi.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: Josefu die yiwo daa dieke die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a dɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ sieŋ, ta die ʋ ka yaala ʋ nagɩ Meri a yi viivi me vuoŋ mana nine me. Naa chɩaŋ ma die ʋ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ ʋ lɔbɩrɩ a zeti wo.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Die ʋ yilinene naa, Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ daansa ma a baarɩ, “Josefu, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Davidi buuriŋ ma, da zeti fʋ balla fʋ nagɩ Meri a bɩrɩŋ fʋ hɔgʋ wa. Dama Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka yɩna wa nyuutiti.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: Ʋ nan mɩɩrɩ bʋadembiŋ aŋ nɩ wa wa Yisa, dama ʋ nan vʋarɩ ʋ vuosi a nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma ma.”

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Die wɩa die Josefu die dɩ hagɩna ʋ daansaha ma die ʋ faarɩ Meri sɩba Ŋmɩŋ malakaka die dɩ bala wa dɩ ʋ yi die wo.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Ama die Josefu aŋaŋ Meri die ka dʋagɩ taŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke die ʋ mɩɩrɩna ʋ bʋadembike. Josefu die dɩ yɩ wa saarɩ, dɩ Yisa.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Die ba mɩɩrɩna Yisa tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Betilehemi, Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma, saŋka mi Herodi die yine naaŋ. Die ba mɩɩrɩna Yisa kʋaŋ chaaŋ die dɩ ka yʋasɩya, dembisi bataŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaaŋ a keŋ Jerusalemi,

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: a dɩ dɩa a pɩasa, “Sɩa ba mɩɩrɩ bʋanyaalɩ dieke dɩ balla ʋ yi Juu vuosisi naaŋ wa? Tɩ ye wo ʋ chɩŋmarɩka dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaaŋ, tɩ dɩ a keŋ dɩ tɩ jɩaŋ wa.”

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Die naaŋ Herodi die dɩ wʋnna naa wa, die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ mana die dɩ benne Jerusalemi ma ma sʋgɩtɩ dɩ chʋʋsɩ.

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: Die ʋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba mana a lagɩŋ tamba a die a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Sɩa ba baa ba mɩɩrɩ gbɩgbatɩtaantʋ Masia wa?”

text: ‘Fʋnɩŋ Betilehemi dɩ benne Judia tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ka yi tɩgɩbiŋ dama fʋ sʋŋ naaŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ baa ʋ nyɩŋ a keŋ a dɩ n vuosisi dɩ benne Izara tɩŋgbaŋka ma nɩŋŋa.’ ”

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Naa chɩaŋ ma die naaŋ Herodi die dɩ wa chaamba die dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩŋ chaakʋ a lɔbɩrɩ a juu juoŋ me a pɩasɩ ba saŋŋa dieke gbaŋ gbaŋ chɩŋmarɩkʋ die dɩ nyɩnna.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”

ref: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
text: Die ba ga a juu tigiri me a ye bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ Meri; die ba sʋʋŋ a gbirigi a jɩaŋ bʋa wa, a yuori ba bʋlɔga a vʋarɩ piini a yɩ wa; die a yiwo salɩma aŋaŋ tulaari dieke ba wasɩnana farankinsinsi aŋaŋ tulaari dieke ba wasɩnana miri.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba ba daansa ma sɩba ba da keŋ yiŋŋi a ga naaŋ Herodi jigiri, die ba nyɩŋ a daagɩ siegaaŋ a kuli ba tɩŋ. Josefu die dɩ chɩgɩna a ga Ijipiti dene

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Die sɩsɩbɩrɩba dɩ yiŋŋine a ga ta Ŋmɩŋ malaka die dɩ nyɩŋ Josefu jigiŋ ʋ daansa ma a die a balɩ wa, “Herodi nan dɩ yaala bʋa wa dɩ ʋ kʋʋ wa. Naa chɩaŋ ma hagɩ a nagɩ bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo a chɩgɩ a ga Ijipiti a bemme mi a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan balɩ fʋ dɩ fʋ nyɩŋ.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Josefu die dɩ hagɩ yuku mi a nagɩ bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo a ga Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: Die ʋ bie wo mi dene a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke naaŋ Herodi die dɩ kpine. Naa die yiye mɩŋ amʋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ vana ʋ naazʋa wʋnyɩ dɩ balɩ die wo keŋ yi wusie, a baarɩ dɩ, “Die n vʋarɩ wa n bʋa Ijipiti me.” Dembibisi kʋʋla wɩa

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Die Herodi die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna dɩ chaaŋ diekemba die dɩ nyɩnna ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩkɩrɩ die gaaŋ wa wa die ʋ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ. Die ʋ yɩ nʋaŋ dɩ ba kʋʋ ballɩnyaalɩ dembisi mana dɩ benne Betilehemi aŋaŋ ka tɩgɩbɩsɩ mana mana. Die ba kʋʋ ballɩ dembisi dieke die dɩ yine ballɩnyaalɩsɩ a ga tʋgɩ bɩna ale ballɩ dama sɩsɩbɩrɩba die baarɩ dɩ ba yene chɩŋmarɩka ka bɩna ale wʋnna.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,

text: “Rama tɩŋ ma ba wʋŋ watɩ, kpaŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ, ta Rachali dɩ kʋma ʋ ballɩ wɩa, ba halɩŋ wa a jɩɩ, dama ʋ ballɩ die kpiye mɩŋ.” Die Josefu aŋaŋ Meri aŋaŋ bʋa wa die dɩ nyɩnna Ijipiti tɩŋgbaŋ ma a yiŋŋi wɩa

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: a baarɩ, “Hagɩ nagɩ bʋa wa aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ wo a yiŋŋi a ga Izara tɩŋgbaŋ ma, dama vuodieke die dɩ yaalala ba kʋʋ wa wa kpiye mɩŋ.”

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Ama die ʋ wʋnna dɩ Akelusi die zene ʋ chɔɔŋ Herodi naŋ ma a dii Judia naarɩ, die ŋmaamɩŋ die yalla wa aŋaŋ ʋ baaŋ nan yiŋŋi a ga mi; ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bɩ a dagɩ wa ʋ daansa sʋŋ dɩ ʋ ga Galili tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Jɔɔn die yeegi wo nyɔgɩnɩ kobiti jayeekiŋ ta bɩ bɔbɩ gbanɩŋ ʋ chɩaŋ ma ta ʋ nyindiike die dɩ yi sankpara aŋaŋ siesi.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: a balla ba tʋntʋmbɩatɩ ta ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ma.

text: Ama die ʋ kenne ye ta Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi, vuosi pam gbaŋ die dɩ kienene ʋ jigiŋ dɩ ʋ sɩɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩnɩŋ nyinvuukehe gie! Mɩnɩa kpaana nɩ dɩ nɩ chɩgɩ nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ sɩnyɩɩrɩ dieke dɩ bala ka keŋ nɩ ma wa?

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Lɩaka wɔŋ yi siri mɩŋ a gbarɩ dɩ ka chɩa tɩɩsɩsɩ; tɩɩ dieke dɩ kana ka nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna ba nan chɩa ka a taaŋ a nagɩ yi boliŋ me.

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ʋ yallɩ wa ʋ kpalɩchaarɩkɩŋ dɩ ʋ chaarɩ zaakpeeti mana mana a nyɩŋ zaaha ma, ʋ nan lagɩsɩ zaaha mana a yi bumbuŋ me ta nagɩ zaakpeetiti a yi boli dieke dɩ kana ka kpise me.” Jɔɔn die dɩ sɩna Yisa Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ dene (Maki

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Ama ta Jɔɔn die dɩ ka saagɩya ta balɩ wa, “Manɩŋ n tɩnna mʋ n keŋ fʋ jigiŋ aŋ fʋ sɩɩ mɩŋ, ama ta fʋnɩŋ fʋ kieŋ n jigiŋ?”

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Womi lɔlɩŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ a baarɩ, “N Bʋachotiŋ wʋnna, wʋnɩŋ n nine dɩ suuli.”

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ wʋnna dɩ ba yigi Jɔɔn a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ, die ʋ nyɩŋ a ga Galili me.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Die ʋ tʋgɩ Nazeriti tɩŋ ama die ʋ ka yʋasɩya mi ta die tɩaŋ ga Kapenɔmi tɩgɩdieke die dɩ gbigine mʋgɩkpɩɩŋ kaanɩ, Zebuluni aŋaŋ Nafitali tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Ta die Sitaani die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ, “Dɩɩ yi fʋ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, vaa tanaha gie bɩrɩŋ nyindiike.”

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, “Ama Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ bɩ a baarɩ, ‘Vuoŋ da a keŋ magɩsɩ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ.’ ”

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Womi Sitaani wa die dɩ bɩ a chii Yisa a yallɩ wa a ga zieŋ taŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ sikpeŋ a dagɩ Yisa dʋnɩa tɩgɩsɩ mana mana aŋaŋ ka nyinti mana ta balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ,

text: “N nan nagɩ nyinti gie mana a yɩ fʋ, dɩɩ yi fʋ keŋ a sʋʋŋ a gbirigi a jɩaŋ mɩŋ.”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: “Zebuluni tɩŋ aŋaŋ Nafitali tɩgɩsɩ dɩ benne Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ mi wo chaaŋ ta bɩ gbigi mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ: Galili tɩŋ dieke vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosisi dɩ kala mi;

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: die ba va ba chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ haarɩkʋ lagɩ lagɩ ta die a dɩ wa. Yisa die dɩ gbaanna yʋagɩtieliŋ dene (Luki

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yisa die dɩ dɩa Galili mana mana sʋŋ a daga ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone sʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa ta die a gbaanna vuodiekemba mana die dɩ yalla yʋagɩtɩ mana mana.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Die ba mʋʋlɩ ʋ saaŋ Siria tɩŋgbaŋ mana mana ma ma. Naa chɩaŋ ma vuosi die dɩ chii vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla yʋagɩtɩ mana mana a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ; jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla bataŋ, bataŋ die dɩ yallɩ kpeŋkpeŋsi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ kpine ta die ʋ gbaaŋ ba mana.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.

text: Vuodiekemba dɩ chɩgɩnana ba chanchaalɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama banɩŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nan chɩgɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Nɩnɩŋ nɩ sɩɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie popoli; tɩŋ dieke ba mɩna a zieŋ kunkogiŋ sikpeŋ ka bɩagɩ a lɔbɩra.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.

Vuodieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ a zɩa ʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ, ‘Gaamʋka gie’ nan daansɩ bie wɩa ma, ta juu boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩ ma.

text: Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a jʋʋsɩ fʋ jaaŋ fʋ yɩ wa ta da a keŋ a mʋŋ wa, vuodieke mana dɩ yaalala ʋ paŋŋɩ jaaŋ fʋ jigiŋ fʋ paŋŋɩ wa da keŋ mʋŋ wa. Dataasɩ wɩa

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: jigidieke fʋ nyinti dɩ benne, mi fʋ sʋŋ dɩaŋ dɩ beri. Nyɩŋgbanɩŋ chaanɩŋ wɩa (Luki

text: “Vuobalɩmɩŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a jɩama naalɩŋ bale. Ʋ nan haa wʋnyɩ ta chome wʋnyɩ, yaa ʋ nan yɩ wʋnyɩ jɩlɩma ta wʋnyɩ ʋ nine kaaŋ dɩ suuli. Vuoŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a jɩama Ŋmɩŋ aŋaŋ ligire.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: “Die wɩa nɩ da vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ ta yilime, ‘Bɩa tɩ nan dii?’ Yaa ‘Bɩa tɩ nan nyuu?’ Yaa ‘Bɩa tɩ nan yeegi?’

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: “Naa chɩaŋ ma wɩa maŋ bala nɩ da vaa ka mugisi nɩ aŋaŋ jadieke nɩ baaŋ nan dii aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan nyuu a bemme nɩ miivoli me; nɩ da bɩ vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ, nɩ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan yeegi jadieke. Mɩsɩ ka tɩaŋ nyindiike? Ta nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ka tɩaŋ nyiŋyeeke?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Nɩ daansɩ a ye nembisi dɩ yʋgɩnana; a ka bʋra; a ka bɩ gobe ta nagɩ yie napooti me, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa daansɩnana ha. Nɩ ka yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ nembisi?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Bɩa yine nɩ mugisi nɩ gbaŋ yaa gamma nyiŋyeeke wɩa? Nɩ daansɩ a ye tɩɩwute aŋaŋ ka vɩɩnɩŋ: ama a ka tʋma a yaala ka wute.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Ama n balɩ nɩ, halɩ naaŋ Solomoni aŋaŋ ʋ nyiŋyeeki vɩɩna mana die ka yeegi jayeekiŋ ka vɩɩna a tʋgɩ tɩɩwutehe gie.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ŋmɩŋ yinene huuti dɩ vɩɩna, nyindieke dɩ benne jinne ta soriŋ ba jʋʋ ha a taaŋ wa, ʋ kaaŋ wa a faasɩ yeegi nɩ nyiŋyeeke? Ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yeegi nɩ? Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba yada dɩ kana a ka dala!

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Vuodieke dɩ kana ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ ŋaana chɩga a yaala nyinti gie mana, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma sɩba a baarɩ dɩ nɩ yaalaha mɩŋ.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: “Nɩ da keŋ a dii nɩ chanchaalɩŋ sarɩya, amʋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ da keŋ dii nɩ sarɩya,

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: dama nɩ dine nɩ chanchaalɩŋ sarɩyaha die wo, die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nan dii nɩ sarɩya; ta nɩ magɩsɩna die a yɩa nɩ chanchaalɩŋ die gbaŋ gbaŋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nan magɩsɩ a yɩ nɩ.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Bɩa yine fʋ daansa yese mɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ chanchaaŋ nimbiŋ me, ta a ka yese dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nimbiŋ me?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Baa fʋ yiwo lalɩa a balɩ a yɩ fʋ nɩmbʋa, ‘Ko vaa n vʋarɩ mɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ nimbiŋ me’ ta daaŋ dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ fʋ nimbiŋ me wɩa?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Fʋnɩŋ gɩgaantʋ wa gie, woliŋ a vʋarɩ dakʋʋlɩ dieke dɩ benne fʋ ninehe me wo amʋ fʋ mɩŋŋɩ a yesime vɩɩnɩŋ a vʋarɩ mɩnchɩɩbɩŋ dieke dɩ benne fʋ nɩmbʋa wa nimbiŋ me wo.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ta dɩɩ yi nɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ gie dɩ sɩba nɩ nagɩ nyɩnvɩɩna a yɩ nɩ ballɩ, die nɩŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma nan faasɩ yɩ vuodieke mana dɩ jʋʋsɩ wa nyɩnvɩɩna.

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Nɩnɩŋ chʋalɩba, nɩ mɩnɩa ʋ bʋa dɩ nan jʋʋsɩ wa nyindiike aŋ ʋ nagɩ taŋ a yɩ wa?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ, ta ʋ nan nagɩ a yɩ nɩ; nɩ daansɩma ta nɩ nan ye; nɩ kpaasɩma gbɩabɩkʋ ta ʋ nan yuori a yɩ nɩ.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ jʋʋsɩnana Ŋmɩŋ tuose mɩŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ yaalɩnana yese mɩŋ; vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ kpaasɩnana gbɩabɩkʋ ka yuore a yɩa wa mɩŋ.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Yaa nɩ mɩnɩa ʋ bʋa nan jʋʋsɩ wa zaasɩŋ aŋ ʋ nagɩ javʋʋkɩŋ a yɩ wa?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: “Yime fʋ chanchaalɩŋ wudieke fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yaala ba yime a yɩma fʋ; dama Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dagɩkʋ dɩ dagɩna die wonde. Sanʋabiŋ juule wɩa (Luki

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: “Naa chɩaŋ ma vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnna manɩŋ n wɩaha gie ta dɩɩsaha nɩasɩ wa dembiyɩantieŋ dieke dɩ mɩna ʋ tigiŋ a zieŋ taŋ sikpeŋ me wo.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Ama miivoli sanʋakʋ yiwo jabɩŋ, ta ka sieku dɩaŋ dɩ tʋa aŋaŋ chɔnɩŋ; ta vuosi bɩta juunene mi. Tɩɩŋ aŋaŋ ka nyɩŋnyɩŋka wɩa (Luki

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: “Nɩ sɩmma ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba wɩa, ba kieŋ nɩ jigiŋ sɩba yiise, ama ba yiwo haaŋgbaasɩ wusie.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ba tʋʋma nan yi nɩ sɩmma ba; ba yese jɩa haŋgɔɔsɩ me a kpaa? Yaa kɩnkama ngbere jigiŋ a kpaa?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Tɩɩvɩɩna mana nyɩna nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna, ama tɩɩ dieke dɩ wone nyʋarɩ mana ŋaaŋ nyɩŋ wa nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩbɩatɩ.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tɩɩvɩɩnɩŋ kaaŋ nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩbɩatɩ; ta tɩɩ dieke dɩ wone nyʋarɩ dɩaŋ kaaŋ nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩvɩɩna.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tɩɩ dieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ kana a ka nyɩna nyɩŋnyɩŋkɩvɩɩna ba nan chɩaka a taaŋ boliŋ me.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Naa chɩaŋ ma nɩ nan dɩ sɩba ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba ba tʋʋma ma. Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ juule wɩa (Luki

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Ama vuodieke mana dɩ wʋnnana manɩŋ n wɩaha gie ta ka dɩɩsaha nɩasɩ wa dembigaamʋ dieke dɩ mɩna ʋ tigiŋ a zieŋ tambʋsɩŋ sikpeku.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Nɩɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ a nɩɩ, mʋga dɩ kpaa, ta bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ nɩgɩ tigiri, tigiri die dɩ nan a ŋmʋʋrɩ!” Yaa gamma Yisa Masia yiko wɩa

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “Da vuodieke mana dɩ wasɩnana mɩŋ, ‘N Yɔmʋtieŋ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ’, balla ʋ juu arɩzanna naarɩ ma, sie vuodiekemba nyɩɩna dɩ yinene n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa choti.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Sarɩya diile daarɩ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ, daadaaŋ pam nan ballɩ a yɩ mɩŋ, ‘N Yɔmʋtieŋ, N Yɔmʋtieŋ, die fʋ saaŋ ma tɩ balla Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, die fʋ saaŋ ma die tɩ yaga jɩmbɩatɩtɩ vuosi ma, die fʋ saaŋ ma die tɩ yie mamachi tʋʋma.’

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Saŋka mi n nan yiŋŋi balɩ ba, ‘N ka sɩba nɩ, nɩnɩŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ vuosi gie, nɩ gamma!’ Mɩmɩɩrɩŋ bale wɩa (Luki

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Nɩɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ a nɩɩ, mʋga dɩ kpaa, ta bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die dɩ faasɩ a nɩgɩ tigiri, ama tigiri die ka nanya, dama die ka zene taŋ ma wa chɩaŋ wɩa.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩna wɩaha gie a kpatɩ, die dɩ yi daadamba mamachi aŋaŋ ʋ dagɩkʋ,

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: dama die ʋ dagɩ sɩba vuodieke dɩ yalla yiko, ta ka dagɩ ba sɩba mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ dagɩnana die wo.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: ama nɩnɩŋ die dɩ yine naarɩ vuosisi, ba nan nagɩ nɩ a vigi taaŋ yeŋ me lɩmɩŋ ma; mi nɩ nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba nɩ nyɩna.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi jɩŋmɩŋ, vuosi die dɩ chii daadaŋ pam die jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla ba a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ; die ʋ yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩtɩ ba ma aŋaŋ jabalɩkɩŋ ta gbaaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ yʋagɩna.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Womi gamɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ a gbigiri ʋ nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, fʋ yaala nan bɩagɩ a gbaaŋ mɩŋ.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Yisa die dɩ tɩɩntɩ ʋ nuuŋ a gbɩ daa wa ta balɩ dɩ, “N saagɩya, fʋ gamɩŋ kpatɩya mɩŋ!” Lele womi ta daa wa dɩ ye alaafɩa.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, “N nan ga a ga gbaaŋ wa.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Yisa die dɩ wʋnna naa die dɩ yi wo mamachi, die ʋ ballɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba die dɩna ʋ kʋaka, “Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, n ye ka ye vuodieke dɩ yine mɩŋ yadaka gie chanchaaŋ nɩnɩŋ Izara vuosi me gbaŋ.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: “Jakʋʋŋ, n tʋntʋntʋ wʋnyɩ paalɩna a yʋagɩ; ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ kpi, ta ʋ die wahala pam, ta dʋa juoŋ me.”

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: N bala nɩ, daadaŋ pam nan nyɩŋ ŋmɩŋ pɔsɩkɩŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩŋ juukiŋ a keŋ ta banɩŋ aŋaŋ Abarahami aŋaŋ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu nan lagɩŋ a kalɩ a die nyindiike arɩzanna ma ma;

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ Romanisisi sojasisi jakʋʋrɩ, “Kulime ta ka nan yi sɩba fʋ yine yada dɩ ka nan yi die wo.” Ta ʋ tʋntʋntʋ wa die dɩ seŋ ye gbaamɩŋ lele womi. Yisa die dɩ gbaanna vuosi pam dene (Maki

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Womi bʋnyɩ die ba faasɩ keese, “Fʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, bɩa fʋ yaala tɩ jigiŋ. Keŋ dɩ fʋ mugisi tɩ mɩŋ ta saŋka mi wa tʋgɩ?”

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Naa chɩaŋ ma, vuoŋ mana die dɩ nyɩŋ tɩka ma a tuole Yisa; die ba yene wo wo die ba jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ ba tɩŋgbaŋka ma.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Die Yisa ga wa Piita tigiŋ, mi die ʋ ye Piita hanɩɩŋ hɔgʋ dɩ yʋagɩna a dʋa aŋaŋ nyɩŋgbaŋ tuuliŋ.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ gbɩ ʋ nuuŋ; ta nyɩŋgbaŋ tuulibu die dɩ kpatɩ, die ʋ hagɩ a yi ba chaantɩ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Die ʋ yiwo naa amʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ balla wudieke wo keŋ a yi wusie dɩ, “Ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ tuo tɩ nyɩŋgbaŋ tuuliŋ aŋaŋ tɩ yʋagɩtɩ a chii a ga.” Vuodiekemba dɩ yaala ba dɩɩ Yisa kʋaŋ wɩa (Luki

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Die Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ, N nan dɩ dɩ fʋ a ga jigidieke mana fʋ ganana.”

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Tɔ, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ keŋ baarɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, vaa n ye woliŋ kuli a ga a guu n chʋa.”

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga sʋgɩrɩ wa a baarɩ, “Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, gbatɩ tɩ a taaŋ, ta tɩ baa tɩ sʋʋŋ wa nyaabʋ chɩaŋ.”

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yisa die dɩ ga a juu haarɩŋ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga.

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Die dɩ ka yʋasɩya ta bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ piili a nɩga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma, nyaabʋ dɩ poŋŋi a juo haarɩkʋ ma a yalla bʋ suuli haarɩkʋ; ama saŋka mi die Yisa die gʋʋra mɩŋ.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Die dɩ yi be mamachi, ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, “Daa lalɩa wʋnna? Halɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ poŋŋiŋ dɩ wʋmma yɩa wa.” Yisa die dɩ gbaanna dembisi bale die jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla ba dene (Maki

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Yisa die dɩ kenne tʋgɩ Gadara tɩŋ ma mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ mi wo chaakʋ, die dembisi bale jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla dɩ nyɩŋ jigidieke ba gunene vuosi a keŋ tuoli wo. Die ba wɩa paalɩ yaa wa ŋmaamɩŋ die wɩa vuoŋ die ka bɩagɩ daaga mi.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Die perukusi a taŋ die ziene a gbigi mi a die.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Jɩmbɩatɩtɩ die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Yisa a baarɩ, “Fʋ keŋ yagɩ tɩ, fʋ vaa tɩ ga juu perukusisi me.”

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ha, “Nɩ gamma.” Die wɩa die a nyɩŋ dembisi bale wo me, a ga a juu perukusisi sʋŋ; Die perukusisi mana dɩ chɩgɩ birrr sʋʋŋ tanɩ chɩaŋ a ga nan mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma ta nyaabʋ dɩ dii he.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Vuodiekemba die dɩ daansɩnana perukusisi die dɩ chɩgɩ a ga tɩka sʋŋ a mʋʋlɩ wudieke die dɩ yine wo, aŋaŋ jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ nyɩna dembisi bale wo me die.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a juu haarɩkʋ a yiŋŋi a jʋalɩ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ mi wo a kuli ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋ ma.

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yisa die dɩ wʋŋ ba pɩasɩkʋ, a die a yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Vuodiekemba dɩ yalla alaafɩa ka yaala gbɩgbaantʋ, sie vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
text: Yaa nɩ yeye ta vuoŋ nagɩ daahaalɩŋ a suu gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋkʋra ma? Dama ʋ yi die, daabʋ nan kʋŋ a chɩɩrɩ kolititi aŋ daabʋ aŋaŋ kolititi mana taaŋ yɔrɩ. Ama ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa daahaalɩŋ a suu gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋhaala aŋ a mana vɩɩŋma.” Jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ havʋʋbiŋ kuŋ wɩa (Maki

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Die ba mʋʋlɩ wɩɩrɩ gie tɩŋgbaŋka ma mana. Yisa die dɩ yuorine yɩɩsɩ bale nine dene

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Womi Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ yile ba sʋgɩtɩ ma dɩ “Daa wa gie bala sɩba ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ.”

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba sʋŋanyile die a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Bɩa nɩ yile sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ die?

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: N nan wa a dagɩ aŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa yaa yiko dʋnɩa ma ma a nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ vuosi tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba”. Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ yʋagɩtieŋ wo, “Hagɩ a nagɩ fʋ kalɩŋŋa a kulime.”

ref: The man got up and went home.
text: Daa wa die dɩ hagɩ a kuli tigiŋ.

ref: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
text: Nɩ gamma Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma a daansɩ ye wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ dɩ, ‘Zɔɔlɩŋ chɩgɩŋ maŋ yaala daa dɔŋɩsɩ kaabɩŋ.’ N ka keŋye dɩ n wa sʋgɩyɩalɩntieliŋ ama n keŋ dɩ n wa wa tʋmbɩatɩtieliŋ.” Nʋabɔbɩŋ wɩa (Maki

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Yisa die dɩ nyɩnna mi a chʋŋ gara dembisi bale die dɩ yine yɩɩsɩ die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ a nata a bala dɩ, “Fʋnɩŋ naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ, chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.”

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
text: Die ba nyɩnnana, vuosi bataŋ die dɩ yaa daa wʋnyɩ die jɩmbɩaŋ die dɩ yalla wa ʋ ka bɩagɩ bala wɩa a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ.

ref: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
text: Yisa die dɩ nyɩnna mi a chʋŋ gara die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ die ba wasɩnana Matiwo, ta ʋ kalɩ ʋ tʋʋma jigiŋ lampotuosiriŋ juoŋ me. Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ wa “Hagɩ a dɩa mɩŋ.” Die ʋ hagɩ dɩ wa.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Yisa die dɩ gana Matiwo tigiri a ga die nyindiikehe, mi lampotuosiriŋ pam aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka dɩ Juu vuosi mɩraha die dɩ keŋ a gʋtɩ Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ma ma a die aŋaŋ ba.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
text: Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ yene die wo die ba pɩasɩ Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, “Bɩa yine nɩ dɩdagɩrʋ wa dɩ die aŋaŋ vuosi gie?”

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: Yisa die dɩ balala naa ta die Juu vuosi jakʋʋŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ a gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, “Lele n havʋʋbiŋ dɩ kpi ama keŋ a nagɩ fʋ nuuŋ a dɩɩsɩ ʋ sikpeŋ aŋ ʋ hagɩ.”

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a ye wo ta baarɩ, “N lɩa, vaa fʋ sʋŋ dʋa, fʋ yada gbaanna fʋ.” Saŋka mi gbaŋ gbaŋ hɔgʋ wa die dɩ ye alaafɩa. Yisa die dɩ vana havʋʋbike dɩ hagɩ kuŋ me dene

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Yisa die dɩ yagɩ jɩmbɩakʋ a nyɩŋ daa wa ma, die ʋ wa bɩagɩ bala wɩa. Die dɩ paalɩ a yi vuoŋ mana mana mamachi ba baarɩ, “Tɩ ye ka ye mamachi ka gie chanchaaŋ Izara vuosi tɩŋ ma ma.”

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Womi Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Bɩa yine tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ Farasisi dɩ bɔba nʋaŋ saŋŋa mana ama ta fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ka wɔŋ a bɔba?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩ tɩŋ yaala chaaŋ diekemba dɩ gana hɔgʋ faarɩŋ bemme aŋaŋ sʋgɩchʋʋsɩŋ saŋŋa dieke ba ŋaaŋ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ dɩ lagɩsɩna a beri wo? Die kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi. Ama saŋŋa nan keŋ ba nan nagɩ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ, saŋka mi ba nan wa bɔbɩ nʋaŋ.

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Vuoŋ kaaŋ saagɩ a nagɩ garɩhaalɩŋ garɩchɔɔtɩŋ a baalɩ a ligi garɩkʋʋŋ vɔrɩŋ, dama garɩhaalɩkʋ gie nan chɩɩrɩ ta vaa garɩkʋʋrɩ, aŋ ka vɔrɩkʋ wa a faasɩ a dalɩma.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Die ʋ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma, “Maŋ gbi ʋ jayeekiri, n nan ye gbaanɩŋ.”

ref: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
text: Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ zɩŋ die dɩ nyɩna ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma dee bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ ale; die dɩ dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ a die a gbi Yisa jayeekiri kʋanʋaŋ.

ref: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
text: Ta die Yisa die dɩ wana a ga jakʋʋrɩ tigiri, a ye hanye chɩarɩba aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kʋnnana kumbu,

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: die ʋ baarɩ “Vuoŋ mana tɩaŋ a nyɩŋ. Havʋʋbike ka kpiye, ʋ ta ko a gʋʋra mɩŋ.” Die vuosi mana die dɩ dɔŋ laatɩ a laa wa.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Die daadamba dɩ wana a nyɩŋ yenni me wo, Yisa die dɩ ga a juu havʋʋbike dɩ dʋana jigidieke a yigi ʋ nuuŋ me die ʋ hagɩ a zie.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ gana a ga a juu juoŋ kaanɩ yɩɩsɩ bale wo die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, die ʋ pɩasɩ ba, “Nɩ tuo a dii dɩ n nan bɩagɩ yi naa?” Ta ba baarɩ, “Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, wa.”

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
text: Die wɩa ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a gbi gbi ba nine ta baarɩ, “Nɩ tuone a dii die wo ka yi a yɩ nɩ.”

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Die ba ninehe die dɩ yuori. Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ, “Nɩ da keŋ a balɩ a yɩ vuoŋ.”

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Ama die ba nyɩŋ a mʋʋlɩ Yisa wɩa tɩka mana ma. Yisa die dɩ gbaanna dembiŋ dieke dɩ kana ka bɩagɩ bala wɩa dene

ref: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
text: Ama ta die Farasisi mba die dɩ baarɩ, “Jɩmbɩatɩtɩ naaŋ wa yɩnana wa hagɩrɩbʋ ʋ bɩagɩ yaga jɩmbɩatɩ.” Yisa die dɩ chɩgɩna vuosisi zɔɔlɩŋ dene

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yisa die dɩ dɩa ga tɩgɩkpɩɩma aŋaŋ tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ mana mana, a die a ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me a daga Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wʋvɩɩna a yɩa vuosi ta gbaama yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri mana.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: Die ʋ yene kpɩkpaakʋ, zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi wo, dama die ba yiwo zɔɔlɩntieliŋ ta wo susuŋŋiŋ mana ta nɩasɩ yiise dɩ wone yiyagɩrʋ.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba, “Zaaha bieye mɩŋ ta dala ama zaagobiribe ka dala.

ref: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
text: Die wɩa nɩ jʋʋsɩ zaaha tieŋ aŋ ʋ gʋtɩ tʋntʋntɩŋ aŋ ba keŋ gobihe.”

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: aŋaŋ Filipi aŋaŋ Batolomiwo; aŋaŋ Tomasi aŋaŋ Matiwo, vuodieke die dɩ yine lampotuosiru wo; aŋaŋ Jemisi vuodieke dɩ yine Alifusi bʋa aŋaŋ Tadeusi,

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Ama ba keŋ yaa nɩ ga aŋ ba dii nɩ sarɩya nɩ da vaa dɩ mugisi nɩ aŋaŋ nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ die yaa nɩ baaŋ nan yiŋŋi ba die; dama saŋka mi Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ nan yɩ nɩ wudieke nɩ baaŋ nan yiŋŋi a balɩ.

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Ama nɩ gamma Izara vuosi jigiŋ nyɩɩna, ba sɩna sɩba yii diekemba dɩ bana.

ref: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
text: “Nɩ wʋmma, n tʋma nɩ sɩba yii diekemba dɩ benne gbaanchʋra sʋnsʋŋ. Die wɩa nɩ yalla yɩaŋ sɩba nyinvuuke ta nɩ bemme sɔmm sɩba ŋmarɩsɩ.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: aŋaŋ Simoni vuodieke dɩ yaala banɩŋ Juu vuosi yalla ba gbaŋ aŋaŋ Judasi Asikaroti, vuodieke die dɩ bala ʋ posi Yisa chɩaŋ. Yisa die dɩ tʋnna ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die (Maki

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: Ta Yisa die dɩ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo ta kpaaŋ ba, “Nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ a ga vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi buuriŋ yaa nɩ ga juu Samaria vuosi tɩŋ ma.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: “Nɩ a keŋ a tʋgɩ tɩgɩkpɩɩŋ yaa tɩgɩbiŋ, nɩ juu a yaalɩ vuodieke dɩ yaalala ʋ tuo nɩ aŋ nɩ bemme ʋ jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ baaŋ nan nyɩŋ mi.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ daa dieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan dii vuosi sarɩya, die tɩka vuosi tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ tɩaŋ Sodomi aŋaŋ Gomoraya vuosi tɩbɩdatɩkʋ. Wahala keniŋ wɩa (Maki

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Saŋka mi vuosi nan nagɩ ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nɩmballɩ a yɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba, chʋalɩŋ gbaŋ nan yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ a yɩ ba ballɩ. Ballɩ dɩaŋ nan yiŋŋi a wagɩ ba chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba nɩɩlɩŋ ta nagɩ ba a yɩ dɩ ba kʋʋ ba.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Nɩ sɩmma nɩ gbaŋ aŋaŋ vuotaŋ wɩa, dama ba nan yigi nɩ a yaa nɩ ga sarɩya diile vuosi jigiŋ, ta bɩ nɩgɩ nɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone sʋŋ.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Ba nan nagɩ nɩ a ga tɩka nyɩŋkʋra aŋaŋ naalɩba jige n saaŋ wɩa aŋ nɩ balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha a yɩ banɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Dama wudiekemba nɩ bala a baa nɩ balɩ wa kaaŋ nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ nan dɩ bie nɩ sʋŋ a bala.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Ta vuosi mana nan dɩ haa nɩ, nɩ dɩna n kʋaŋ wɩa. Ama vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a bɩagɩ a tuoli wɩaha a ga a tʋgɩ kpatɩkʋ nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Ta ba keŋ mugisi nɩ tɩŋ kaanɩ ma, nɩ chɩgɩ a ga tɩgɩgaaŋ. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ kpatɩ nɩ tʋʋmaha Izara tɩgɩsɩ mana ma ta manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa keŋ.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Bʋbʋgɩrʋ ka tɩaŋ ʋ dɩdagɩrʋ; yɔmʋ dɩaŋ ka tɩaŋ ʋ tieŋ.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Die wɩa dɩ mʋ aŋ bʋbʋgɩrʋ sɩmma sɩba ʋ dɩdagɩrɩ wa; ta yɔmʋ dɩaŋ sɩɩ sɩba ʋ tieŋ wo. Ba a keŋ a wa tigiŋ tieŋ jɩmbɩatɩ naaŋ wa Beelizebuli, die nɩŋ lalɩa ba baa ba wa ʋ dembe? Mɩnɩa mʋna dɩ tɩ chɩgɩma wa ŋmaamɩŋ? (Luki

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Nɩ da keŋ a chɩgɩma vuodiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a kʋʋ nɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka, ama ta kaaŋ bɩagɩ a kʋʋ nɩ haalɩbʋ; ama nɩ chɩgɩma Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a kʋʋ nɩ nyɩŋgbaŋka aŋaŋ nɩ haalɩbʋ mana boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩkʋ ma.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Ba daa nembisi ale wo kobu, ama dɩ ka yi tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ, a kaanɩ mana kaaŋ nan tɩŋgbaŋ.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ sɩba nɩ zoosi nʋaŋ mɩŋ.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Die wɩa nɩ da chɩgɩma ŋmaamɩŋ; dama nɩ yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ nembisi pam. Vʋarɩ fʋ gbaŋ yaalɩŋ ma dɩ yiwo Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ (Luki

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: “Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a vʋarɩ ʋ gbaŋ vuosi me dɩ ʋ yiwo n vuoŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ nan vʋarɩ wa yaalɩŋ ma n Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Ama vuodieke dɩ baarɩ ʋ ka yi n vuoŋ vuosi jigiŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ nan balɩ dɩ n ka sɩba wa n Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ. Puole wɩa (Luki

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Nɩ da keŋ a yilime sɩba sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ maŋ yaa keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi jigiŋ; Aayɩ, n ka keŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ama n keŋ wo aŋaŋ puole.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: N keŋ dɩ n yiwo ballɩ dembisi tuoli ba chʋalɩŋ, ta havʋʋbisi tuoli ba nɩɩlɩŋ, lɩahaalʋ tuoli ʋ nuŋ.

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Vuota dataasɩ nan yi ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tigiŋ vuosi.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Vuodieke mana dɩ chone ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ a tɩaŋ mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ; vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ chone ʋ naahaalʋ yaa ʋ havʋʋbiŋ a tɩaŋ mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Vuodieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ kana ka vigi ʋ dagarɩkɩŋ a dɩa mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Vuodieke mana dɩ yaalala dɩ ʋ gbatɩ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ miivoli nan waarɩ ka; ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ waarɩ ʋ miivoli n wɩa wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ nan daansɩ ye ke. Vuodiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan daansɩ ye tune wɩa (Maki

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Vuodieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ a keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a tuo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa chaantɩ ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wɩa, nan daansɩ tuo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa tune; vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tuo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ chaantɩ ʋ yine sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ wɩa, nan daansɩ tuo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ tune.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: “Vuodieke mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a tuo nɩ chaantɩ, mɩŋŋɩ tuo mɩŋ mɩŋ; ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ mɩŋŋɩna tuo mɩŋ, mɩŋŋɩ tuo wo Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ tʋnna mɩŋ wa mɩŋ.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a yuori nyasʋnsʋŋ a yɩ n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gie wʋnyɩ dɩ ʋ yine n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wɩa nan daansɩ ye tune.”

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Die Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋine a gara wa, Yisa die dɩ piili a bala yɩa kpɩkpaakʋ Jɔɔn wɩa, “Die nɩ nyɩnna a ga Jɔɔn jigiŋ haagɩrɩ ma wa, bɩa die nɩ tɩŋ a yaala nɩ ye? Die nɩ tɩŋ yile a baarɩ nɩ ye hʋʋtɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ dɩ nɩgɩnana?

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Jɔɔn die dɩ keŋ ta bɔba nʋaŋ ta ka nyuu daaŋ vuosi dɩ baarɩ dɩ, ‘Jɩmbɩaŋ yalla wa.’

ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Die Yisa dɩ dagɩna wɩaha gie a yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a kpatɩ wa die ʋ nyɩŋ mi a ga tɩgɩ diekemba die dɩ gbigine mi a bala ta daga Ŋmɩŋ wɩa mi.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die bie wo dansarɩka sʋŋ, ta die a wʋŋ Yisa Masia tʋʋma wɩa; die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ bataŋ Yisa jigiŋ

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: dɩ ba ga pɩasɩ wa, “Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ keŋ wo yaa tɩ chɩsɩma vuogaaŋ bɩbra?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Nɩ yiŋŋi a ga balɩ Jɔɔn wudieke nɩ wʋnnana ta yese wudiekemba;

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: dɩ nɩ ye ta yɩɩsɩ nine dɩ yuorine, gbarɩgɩsɩ dɩ chʋŋ, ta vuodiekemba die dɩ yʋagɩna gamɩŋ ye wo gbaamɩŋ, tɩkpaara tieŋ dɩ wʋmma, ta kunti dɩ hagɩna kuŋ me, ta zɔɔlɩntieliŋ dɩ wʋmma Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkɩ vɩɩnaha.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Ta sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka chɩɩlɩya mɩŋ ma.”

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Bɩa die nɩ nyɩŋ ga nɩ ye? Daa dɩ yeegine nyiŋyeekivɩɩna? Vuodieke dɩ yeegine nyiŋyeekivɩɩna bie wo naalɩsɩ ma.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Nɩ balɩ mɩŋ, bɩa die nɩ nyɩŋ ga nɩ ye? Die nɩ yile sɩba nɩ nan ye Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa? Wusie, n bala nɩ, nɩ ye vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Ta Yisa dɩ bɩ a balɩ ba dɩ, “Wusie maŋ bala nɩ ba ye ka mɩɩrɩ vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, ama vuodieke dɩ yine vuobiŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma tɩaŋ wa mɩŋ.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: A nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Jɔɔn die dɩ balala Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha gie a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, vuodiekemba nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩna Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ juule wɩa, pɔgɩlɩha aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba juu aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: Ta nɩ a keŋ a yaala nɩ tuo wɩaha dii, Jɔɔn yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Elaja, vuodieke die dɩ balla ʋ keŋ wo.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Lele, bɩa maŋ baa n nagɩ jinne vuosi gie a magɩsɩ? Ba nɩasɩ wa ballɩbɩsɩ dɩ kalla nyʋʋgɩtɩ jige, a wasa tamba.

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: ‘Tɩ wɩwa wɩɩŋ a yɩ nɩ ama nɩ ka sieye, ta tɩ chɩa hanye a yɩ nɩ ama nɩ ka kʋŋya.’

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Ta manɩŋ vuota Bʋa die dɩ keŋ, a die ta nyuo, vuosi dɩ baarɩ, ‘Nɩ ye daa wa gie ʋ yiwo wutulitieŋ aŋaŋ daanyuru, ta yi lampotuosiriŋ aŋaŋ vuobɩatɩ zʋa.’ Ama Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋnna die wo dagɩya sɩba ʋ yaa yɩaŋ vɩɩnɩŋ.” Tɩŋ diekemba yada dɩ kana ka dala wɩa (Luki

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Vuodiekemba die dɩ benne tɩgɩ diekemba ma ma Yisa die dɩ yine ʋ mamachi wɩa die, ta ka chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma, die wɩa die ʋ galɩŋ ba.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: Ta nɩnɩŋ Kapenɔmi vuosi aŋaŋ nɩ kɔtɩna nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ mana, Ŋmɩŋ nan vigi nɩ a taaŋ kunti jigiberisikiŋ dama mamachi diekemba die n yine nɩ jigiri die dɩ tɩŋ yi Sodomi ma ma dɩ mʋna naa a tɩŋ ye beri a ga tʋgɩ jinne.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Ama n bala nɩ, sarɩyaka diile daraaŋ nɩ tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ dala a tɩaŋ Sodomi vuosi tɩbɩdatɩŋ.” Nɩ keŋ n jigiŋ a voosi (Luki

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Saŋka mi Yisa die dɩ baarɩ, “N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ yine ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie tieŋ, n bɩra fʋ, dama fʋnɩŋ lɔbɩrɩ wɩaha gie yɩantieliŋ aŋaŋ sɩsɩbɩrɩŋ jigiŋ ama ta yuori he a yɩ ballɩbɩsɩ;

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: dama die fʋ tʋma fʋ tʋʋma a yie fʋ nansɩŋ.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “N Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ wa jaaŋ mana a yɩ mɩŋ; vuoŋ ka sɩba manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa sie n Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ, vuoŋ dɩaŋ ka sɩba n Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ sie manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa wa aŋaŋ vuodiekemba n yaala n nagɩ wa a dagɩ.

ref: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
text: “Nɩ keŋ n jigiŋ nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ chiine chiidʋnsa aŋ n yɩ nɩ voosiŋ.

ref: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
text: Nɩ nagɩ n chiiti a chii ta nɩ bʋgɩrɩ a nyɩŋ n ma dama n yiwo vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna n gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ta mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ n gbaŋ ta nɩ nan ye voosiŋ.

ref: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.
text: Dama n kʋaŋ dɩɩsɩŋ ka tʋa ta n chiiti dɩaŋ dɩ ka dʋnsɩ.”

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ yene die wo ba balɩ a yɩ Yisa, “Daansɩ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ yinene wudieke tɩ mɩraha dɩ kana ka yɩ sieŋ dɩ tɩ yime davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.”

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
text: Die dɩ yi daadamba mana mamachi aŋaŋ Yisa die dɩ yine die wo wɩa, die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, “Daa wa gie yiwo Naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ yaa?”

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Ama manɩŋ nɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka yinene maŋ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma; die dagɩna sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ keŋ nɩ jigiŋ mɩŋ. Yisa dɩ bɩagɩna a bɔbɩ Sitaani die

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Yaa nɩ ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha gbaŋkʋ ma, dɩ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ mana Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ benne Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma ŋaaŋ tʋma tʋʋma mi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ mana a chʋsa mɩraha ama ta wo chʋʋsɩŋ.

ref: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
text: Die ʋ ga a juu wo Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋanvuosisi dɩ nagɩ paanʋ dieke die ba nagɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ a ŋɔbɩ wudieke die Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ kana ka yɩ sieŋ dɩ vuogaaŋ ŋɔbɩ, die paanʋ sie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩŋkʋra nyɩɩna.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ ma dɩ, ‘Ŋmɩŋ yaala zɔɔlɩnchɩgɩrɩŋ a tɩaŋ nɩ kaaba.’ Nɩ tɩŋ sɩba wɩɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ, nɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ galɩma vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka chʋʋsɩya;

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Yisa die nyɩŋ mi a ga juu ba Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoŋ kaanɩ ma.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ nuukpiikiri tieŋ dɩ, “Tɩɩrɩ fʋ nuuke.” Die ʋ tɩɩrɩ ka die ka mana dɩ tɩɩntɩ, a sɩɩ sɩba kaanɩ wa.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Die Yisa die dɩ sɩbɩna ba saŋkʋ wɩaha die ʋ nyɩŋ mi, ta vuosi pam die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ. Die ʋ gbaaŋ yʋagɩtieliŋ mana,

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: ta kpaaŋ ba dɩ ba da keŋ a balɩ a yɩ ba chanchaalɩŋ ʋ wɩa.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Die ʋ yiwo naa amʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ bala die wo keŋ yi wusie.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Nɩ ye, n bʋavʋarɩkɩŋ wʋnna, wʋnɩŋ maŋ faasɩ cho, ʋ ma n sʋŋ dɩ fɩalɩ. N nan nagɩ n Halɩkasɩka a yi ʋ ma, aŋ ʋ balɩ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka yi Juu vuosi dɩ ba nan ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ n jigiŋ.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: “Vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka zie n kʋaŋ waga mɩŋ mɩŋ; ta vuodieke mana dɩ kana ka suŋŋi mɩŋ a lagɩsa vuosi n jigiŋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ jatɩnana ba.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Womi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ chii daa wʋnyɩ dɩ yine yɩɩ aŋaŋ gaamʋ dama jɩmbɩaŋ die yalla wa; ta Yisa die dɩ gbaaŋ wa, die ʋ bɩagɩ a bala wɩa ta yese.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ ba yilinene wudieke ta yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Tɩŋgbaŋ dieke mana dɩ puonene ka gbaŋ a waga taŋ kaaŋ faasɩ a yʋasɩ. Tigidieke dɩaŋ yaa buuri dieke dɩ puonene ka gbaŋ a waga taŋ nan nan.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Dɩ yiwo Beelizebuli yɩnana mɩŋ yiko maŋ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi ma, ta nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ mɩnɩa yɩnana nɩ vuosisi yiko dɩ ba vʋarɩmaha? Nɩ vuosi gbaŋ gbaŋ tʋʋma daga sɩba nɩ wo wusie.

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
text: “Dɩɩ yi ta jɩmbɩaŋ dɩ keŋ nyɩŋ vuota ma, ka ŋaaŋ dɩa wa jigikʋʋkɩŋ a yaala jigiŋ dɩ ka voosi, ka ka yeye;

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
text: Ka ŋaaŋ wa nyɩŋ mɩŋ a ga a chii jɩmbɩatɩ diekemba dɩ tɩanna ka gbaŋ gbaŋ ayʋpɔyɩ aŋ a ga bemme mi. Naa chɩaŋ ma daa wa beriŋ nan faasɩ chʋʋsɩ pam a tɩaŋ die ʋ sɩna die wo. Naa bala ka yi a yɩ lele gie vuobɩatɩtɩ.” Yisa nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ wɩa (Maki

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: Ta Yisa die dɩ bɩ balɩ dɩ, “Dɩɩ yi ta tɩɩŋ dɩ vɩɩna, ka nyɩna nyɩŋnyɩŋka vɩɩna; ama dɩɩ yi ta tɩɩŋ dɩ ka vɩɩna, ka ŋaaŋ nyɩŋ wa nyɩŋnyɩŋka bɩatɩ dama tɩɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka ma ba sɩba ka.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Nɩnɩŋ nyinvuuke buuribu gie, nɩ yine vuobɩatɩ die, lalɩa wʋbalɩkɩ vɩɩna dɩ nan bɩagɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ nʋa ma? Dama jadieke vuota dɩ yiline ʋ sʋŋ ma die nyɩnnana ʋ nʋaŋ ma.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: “N bala nɩ, sarɩya diile daraaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan dii vuosi sarɩya sɩba die ba balala wʋtɔgɩtɩ mana wɩa,

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: dama vuoŋ mana wʋbalɩka bala ka vʋarɩ wa dɩnda daarɩ mi yaa ʋ wʋbalɩka dɩaŋ bala ka chʋʋsɩ wa.” Die ba yaalala ba ye mamachi wɩa (Maki

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Womi mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ aŋaŋ Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ tɩ yaala fʋ yiwo mamachi wɩɩŋ aŋ tɩ ye.”

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Ama die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ, “Lele vuosi dɩ yine vuobɩatɩ ta ka bɩ jɩama Ŋmɩŋ yaalala ba ye mamachi wɩɩŋ; ama n kaaŋ wɔŋ yi mamachi wɩɩŋ a yɩ ba; Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Jona dagɩŋ chanchaaŋ nyɩɩna maŋ nan dagɩ ba.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Die Jona dɩ benne zaasɩkpɩɩkʋ sʋkʋ ma ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ a ga tʋgɩ daraa ataa wa, die gbaŋ gbaŋ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩaŋ dɩ nan dɩ beri tɩŋgbaŋka gie chɩaŋ ŋmɩntʋasɩ aŋaŋ yuŋ a ga tʋgɩ daraa ataa.

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Sarɩya diile daraaŋ Nineva vuosi nan hagɩ zie a galɩŋ nɩ, dama Jona dɩ mʋʋlɩna Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩ ba wa die ba va ba tʋmbɩatɩ mɩŋ; n bala nɩ, vuodieke dɩ tɩanna Jona benne gie.

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Sarɩya diile daraaŋ Siaba hɔgʋ naaŋ wa nan hagɩ a zie a galɩŋ nɩ dama die ʋ paalɩ nyɩŋ wa tɩgɩ saasaa a ga dɩ ʋ wʋŋ naaŋ Solomoni yɩaŋ wʋbalɩkaha, ama n bala nɩ vuodieke dɩ tɩanna naaŋ Solomoni benne giena. Jɩmbɩaŋ dɩ yiŋŋine keŋ juo vuoŋ ma dene (Luki

ref: it says, “I will go back to the home I left.” When it gets there and finds the place empty, clean, and neat,
text: ʋ ŋaaŋ balɩ a yɩ wa ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ dɩ, ‘N nan yiŋŋi a ga n tigidieke n vana wa.’ Die wɩa ka ŋaaŋ yiŋŋi ga mi a ye dɩ vuoŋ wo mi, ta ka sʋŋ mana saarɩya a wʋnsɩ vɩɩnɩŋ.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Yisa die ye ko bala wɩa a yɩa vuotamba ta ʋ nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ. Die ba zie wo yeŋ me ta tʋŋ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ wa wa aŋ ba balɩ wɩa.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Die wɩa vuoŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩ wa dɩ, “Daansɩ ye, fʋ naa aŋaŋ fʋ nɩmballɩ zie yeŋ me ta yaala ba aŋaŋ fʋ balɩ wɩa.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, “Mɩnɩa yine n naa? Mɩnɩa yine n nɩmballɩ?”

ref: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
text: Womi ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuuŋ a dɩɩŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ta baarɩ, “Ye, n naa aŋaŋ n nɩmballɩ wʋnna.

ref: Anyone who obeys my Father in heaven is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ yinene wudieke n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ benne arɩzanna ma choti wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine n nɩmbʋa aŋaŋ n taa aŋaŋ n naa.”

ref: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Ta daadamba die dɩ geline wo die faasɩ dala mɩŋ, die wɩa die ʋ ga juu haarɩŋ a kalɩ ka sʋŋ, ta die daadamba die dɩ zie mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ kʋanʋarɩ ma.

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Die ʋ nagɩ wa nandaga a balɩ ba wɩa pam dɩ, “Daa wʋnyɩ die hagɩna daaŋ kaanɩ a ga dɩ ʋ bʋrɩ ʋ nyɩŋbʋra.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan taŋ sikpeŋ jigidieke tantɩ die dɩ kana ka faasɩ dala. Die nyɩŋbʋrɩkaha die dɩ nyʋʋŋ lagɩ lagɩ dama tantɩ die ka faasɩ dala wa wɩa,

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Ta ataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ nan haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ ma, ta a mana die dɩ lagɩsɩ nyʋʋŋ a bɩrɩŋ, ama haŋgɔɔsɩsɩ die dɩ nyagɩ nyɩŋbʋraha.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Die wɩa die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Bɩa wɩa fʋ naga nandaga bala yɩa ba?”

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Vuodieke dɩ sɩbɩna Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yenene a gʋta aŋ ʋ faasɩ yallɩma pam, ama vuodieke dɩ wone, ʋ nan waarɩ bɩta dieke ʋ yalla gbaŋ.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.

text: Ʋ die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Tɩɩ dieke mana n Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩ kana ka suuye sie ba keŋ vʋʋka a taaŋ.

ref: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
text: “Dɩ yiwo lalɩa fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ka dɩɩsa tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ dagɩna die wo? Ba ka nɩta ba nuusi ta die.”

ref: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba, “Ta dɩ yiwo lalɩa nɩnɩŋ nɩ zeti Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha ta dɩ nɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba dagɩkʋ?

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Ama nɩ daga a baarɩ dɩ vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a yallɩ jadieke ʋ baaŋ nan nagɩ a suŋŋi ʋ nuŋ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ ta baarɩ dɩ ‘Nyinti gie bɩrɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ’;

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: ka ka bɩ yiwo mugisiŋ dɩ ʋ yɩma ba jɩlɩma ta yɩ ba jaaŋ. Naa nɩ zeti Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha ta dɩ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ dagɩŋ.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
text: Ba jɩama mɩŋ wa yɔrɩ dama ba daga vuosi vuota mɩra sɩba a yiwo manɩŋ n mɩra.’ ” Jadieke dɩ benne vuota sʋŋ ma wɩa (Maki

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Die wɩa vaa ba aŋ ba ga dama ba sɩɩ sɩba yɩɩsɩ ta data taŋ. Yɩɩ dɩ a keŋ data ʋ chanchaaŋ sie ba mana nan guoliŋ me.”

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Ama Yisa die ka balɩ wɩɩŋ mana a yɩ wa. Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ ʋ yagɩ wa; dama ʋ dɩ tɩ mɩŋ a yie watɩ.

ref: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Da jadieke dɩ junene vuota nʋaŋ ma yinene wo dɩgɩntɩ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ ama jadieke dɩ nyɩnana ʋ nʋaŋ ma yinene wo dɩgɩntɩ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ.”

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Womi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Fʋ sɩba a baarɩ Farasisi vuosisi dɩ wʋnna fʋ balɩkʋ ba jɩɩ wa sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ?”

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Piita die yiŋŋi a baarɩ: “Dagɩ tɩ nandagɩrɩ gie chɩaŋ.”

ref: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ka sɩba n wʋbalɩka gie chɩasɩ?

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Nɩ ka sɩba a baarɩ jadieke mana dɩ junene vuota nʋaŋ ma gara ʋ nyuuti ma ta daaga ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ a nyɩna?

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Ama wudiekemba vuota dɩ balala ʋ nʋaŋ ma nyɩna ʋ sʋŋ, die yinene vuota dɩgɩntɩ.

ref: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
text: Dama vuota sʋŋ ma sʋŋanyilibɩatɩ dɩ nyɩna, sɩba vuota kʋʋla, daakpana aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana yaalɩŋ, aŋaŋ gaarɩŋ aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ aŋaŋ kʋagaarɩŋ.

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: Genhe yinene vuota dɩgɩntɩ; ama da nuudɩgɩntɩ diile yinene vuota dɩgɩntɩ.” Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ yine Yisa yada wɩa (Maki

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, “Izara vuodiekemba dɩ sɩna sɩba yii diekemba dɩ bana, banɩŋ ba jigiŋ nyɩɩna Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tʋŋ mɩŋ.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Ta kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ aŋaŋ gbilisi aŋaŋ yɩɩsɩ aŋaŋ gbarɩgɩsɩ aŋaŋ gaantɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba mana dɩ yʋagɩna yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri mana a nagɩ ba dʋaŋ ʋ nɩŋŋa; die ʋ gbaaŋ ba.

ref: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
text: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga Taya aŋaŋ Sidoni tɩŋgbaŋ chaaŋ.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Die wɩa Keenani hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ benne mi chaakʋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a wa wa dɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ Davidi haagɩŋ chɩgɩ mɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ! Jɩmbɩaŋ yalla n havʋʋbiŋ, dɩ a paalɩ a mugisi wo pam.”

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Ta hɔgʋ wa die dɩ a keŋ a gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, suŋŋi mɩŋ!”

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, “Dɩ ka mʋ dɩ vuoŋ nagɩ ballɩbɩsɩ nyindiike a yɩ gbaasɩ.”

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Die wɩa hɔgʋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ ka yiwo wusie, ama gbaaŋ diekemba dɩ dʋana ba tieŋ nɩŋŋa ŋaaŋ nagɩ nyindiike chɔɔta dieke dɩ nannana ʋ nɩŋŋa a die.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, “Yiwo hɔgʋ dieke yada dɩ dala! Fʋ yaalala jadieke wo nan yi a yɩ fʋ.” Lele womi ʋ hɔgʋlɩa wa dɩ ye alaafɩa. Yisa die dɩ gbaanna vuosi pam dene

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ chaaŋ. Die a jʋalɩ a kalɩ kunkogiŋ me.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ yene ta gaantɩba balɩ wɩa, ta gbarɩgɩsɩ dɩ chʋŋ, ta gbilisi dɩ ye alaafɩa, yɩɩsɩ dɩ yese wo die dɩ yi be mamachi. Die ba bɩrɩ Izara vuosi Ŋmɩŋ. Yisa die dɩ yɩna dembisi tuse anɩɩsa nyindiike dene (Maki

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Yisa die dɩ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, “Zɔɔlɩŋ yigi mɩŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ vuosi gie wɩa, dama ba bie wo n jigiŋ daraa ataa, ta lele ba wo jaaŋ ba baaŋ dii. N ka yaala n vaa ba kuli ta ka yɩ ba nyindiike, dama ba nan keŋ chaa sieŋ me.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ pɩasɩ wa, “Sɩa tɩ nan ye nyindiike haagɩrɩ gie me a yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ gie?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba, “Paanʋsɩ aŋmɩa nɩ yallɩ?” Ba baarɩ, “Paanʋsɩ golime ayʋpɔyɩ aŋaŋ zaasɩbɩsɩ bɩta.”

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa Yisa die dɩ yi kpɩkpaakʋ nʋaŋ dɩ ba sʋʋŋ kala tɩŋgbaŋ.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Die ʋ nagɩ paanʋ golime ayʋpɔyɩ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩbɩsɩsɩ a bɩrɩ Ŋmɩŋ ta die gbieri gbieri he a nagɩ ha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die ba nagɩha a yɩ vuosisi.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Die ba mana dɩ dii ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ. Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩ nyintɩalɩkaha a suuli kparɩsɩ ayʋpɔyɩ.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Die Yisa dɩ wa a taaŋ kpɩkpaakʋ ta die juu haarɩŋ a ga Magadani tɩŋgbaŋ chaaŋ ma.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Bɩa yine nɩ yile dɩ paanʋ wɩa maŋ bala nɩ? ‘Nɩ sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi dabɔtɩ wɩa!’ ”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Yisa die dɩ ga Siisiria Filipi tɩŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, “Mɩnɩa vuosisi dɩ baarɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa yiye?”

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: Die ba wa a sɩba a baarɩ dɩ ʋ ka bala ba dɩ ba sɩmma yaa gamma dabɔtɩ dieke ba yalla a yɩa paanʋ sʋŋ ama dɩ ba sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi dagɩŋ wɩa. Piita dɩ bala wʋkpɩɩma yaa gamma Yisa ma dene (Maki

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Ama die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, “Nɩɩŋmara dɩ keŋ ŋmɩntɩ jɩŋmɩŋ nɩ ŋaaŋ baarɩ, ‘Soriŋ jigiri nan dɩ vɩɩna.’

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Ta sʋkʋleeliŋ dɩaŋ nɩɩŋmara dɩ keŋ ŋmɩntɩ ta bɩ sɩbɩ nɩ ŋaaŋ baarɩ, ‘Nɩɩŋ baa ka nɩɩŋ mɩŋ.’ Nɩ nan bɩagɩ a daansɩ nɩɩŋmara ta balɩ wudieke dɩ balla ka yi ama nɩ kaaŋ dɩ sɩba wudieke dɩ yinene lele chɩasɩ!

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Die ba baarɩ, “Bataŋ baarɩ yiwo Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, bataŋ dɩaŋ baarɩ yiwo Elaja ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ baarɩ yiwo Jerimaya yaa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ benne kʋrɩŋ ma wa.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: A nyɩŋ saŋka mi yaa gamma, Yisa die dɩ piili a bala a yuore ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, “Sie n ga Jerusalemi a ga dii wahala a nyɩŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba nuusi me. Ba nan kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ Ŋmɩŋ nan hagɩ mɩŋ a nyɩŋ kuŋ me.”

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba, “Nɩ sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Sadusisi dabɔtɩ wɩa.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Die ba a bala a yɩa taŋ a baarɩ, “Dɩ yiwo tɩ kana ka nagɩ paanʋ wɩa ʋ bala die wo.”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Dama manɩŋ vuota Bʋa baa n yiŋŋi keŋ mɩŋ aŋaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ hagɩrɩŋ, aŋaŋ ʋ malakasi, ta saŋŋa mi n nan tuŋ vuoŋ mana yaa gamma ʋ tʋnna die.

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: N bala fʋ, fʋnɩŋ fʋ yiwo Piita ta tanjaalɩka gie sikpeŋ maŋ nan nagɩ n vuosi zieŋ, ta kuŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a nyaŋŋɩ ba.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Ta ʋ pɩasɩ ba, “Ta nɩnɩŋ mɩnɩa nɩ baarɩ n yiye?”

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Womi Simoni Piita dɩ baarɩ, “Fʋ yine miivolitieŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa Masia wa, vuodieke ʋ vʋarɩna.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, “Fʋnɩŋ Simoni, Jona bʋa, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bemme fʋ ma, dama daa vuota yuori wɩarɩ gie a dagɩ fʋ ama dɩ yiwo n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: N nan yɩ fʋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sanʋaŋ safɩbie; die wɩa jadieke mana fʋ baarɩ dɩ chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me die gbaŋ gbaŋ jaabʋ nan dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma; ta jadieke mana fʋ baarɩ ka ka chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, die gbaŋ gbaŋ jaabʋ kaaŋ dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba da keŋ balɩ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ yine Masia wa. Yisa die dɩ bala ʋ wahala aŋaŋ ʋ kuŋ wɩa dene (Maki

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Piita die dɩ nagɩ wa a nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ a piili a galɩma wa a baarɩ dɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, Ŋmɩŋ da vaa die yi. Naa kaaŋ daaŋ yi fʋ.”

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ Piita, “Fʋnɩŋ Sitaani wa gie hagɩ gie ta va mɩŋ. Yiwo taŋgbiŋkiŋ n sieŋ me dama fʋ sʋŋanyilehe gie yiwo vuota sʋŋanyile ama daa Ŋmɩŋ.”

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Womi ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, “Vuodieke mana dɩ yaala ʋ dɩa n kʋaŋ, sie ʋ zeti ʋ gbaŋ a nagɩ ʋ dagarɩkɩŋ a dɩa mɩŋ.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Dama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka saagɩya dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ miivoli a yɩ mɩŋ nan waarɩ ka, ama vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ keŋ waarɩ ʋ miivoli n wɩa wʋnɩŋ nɩŋ nan daansɩ ye ke.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: Bɩa vuota dɩ baa ʋ ye, ʋ yallɩ dʋnɩa nyinti mana ta waarɩ ʋ miivoli, bɩa nyʋarɩ ʋ baa ʋ ye? Vuoŋ nan bɩagɩ nagɩ jaaŋ a daa ʋ miivoli?

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuosi bataŋ bie gie ba kaaŋ kpi sie ba keŋ ye manɩŋ vuota Bʋa naarɩ diile saŋŋa.”

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Womi ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ tarɩgɩ ba nɩŋŋa. Ʋ nine dɩ chaaŋ sɩba ŋmɩŋ ta ʋ nyiŋyeeke dɩ yɩantɩ sɩba chaanɩŋ.

ref: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
text: Womi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a baarɩ Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ wɩa ʋ bala die. Yisa die dɩ vʋarɩna jɩmbɩaŋ dembiŋ ma dene (Maki
