ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Daraa ayʋaba kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa die dɩ nagɩ Piita, aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn, a yaa ba ga jʋalɩ taŋbɩrɩŋkɩŋ sikpeŋ me, ba nyɩɩna.

ref: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
text: Ta ba ye Mosisi aŋaŋ Elaja dɩ nyɩnna bʋnyɩ ba jigiŋ a bala wɩa aŋaŋ Yisa.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Die wɩa Piita die dɩ balɩ a yɩ Yisa dɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ vɩɩna tɩ benne giena. Fʋ tɩŋ nan saagɩ, n tɩŋ nan kpii viisi ataa giena, ta kaanɩ fʋ jaaŋ, ta kaanɩ yi Mosisi jaaŋ ta kaanɩ dɩaŋ dɩ yi Elaja jaaŋ.”

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Die Piita die dɩ yene ko bala wɩaha, nɩɩŋmaayɩalɩŋ dɩ keŋ ligi be, ta lɔlɩŋ dɩ nyɩŋ nɩɩŋmaakʋ ma a baarɩ, “N Bʋachoti wʋnna, ʋ ma n sʋŋ dɩ paalɩ a fɩalɩ, nɩ wʋmma a yɩma wa.”

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna lɔlɩkʋ die ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ faasɩ a yigi be, die ba gbirigi tɩŋgbaŋ.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Yisa die dɩ keŋ ba jigiŋ a gbi gbi ba ta baarɩ, “Nɩ hagɩ zie. Da nɩ chɩgɩma ŋmaamɩŋ.”

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Die wɩa die ba kɔtɩ ba sikpigile a daansɩ ta ka ye vuoŋ mi, sie Yisa nyɩɩna ma.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Bɩa wɩa mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba dɩ baarɩ dɩ sie Elaja woliŋ keŋ Masia ma?”

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, “Ka yiwo wusie dɩ sie Elaja woliŋ keŋ, amʋ ʋ wʋnsɩ wɩɩŋ mana a mɩŋŋɩ zieŋ.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Ama manɩŋ n bala nɩ dɩ, Elaja wɔŋ keŋ mɩŋ ama ta vuosi dɩ ka mɩŋŋɩ wa, ta yi wo yɔrɩ yɔrɩ die ba yaalala die wo. Die gbaŋ gbaŋ ba nan yi manɩŋ vuota Bʋa aŋ n dii wahala ba nuusi me.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataa wa dɩ yiŋŋine a ga kpɩkpaakʋ jigiri daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ a gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa,

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: a baarɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ chɩgɩ n bʋa dembike gie zɔɔlɩŋ, dama ʋ yiwo kpenkpennantʋ, die dɩ paalɩ a mugisi wo a yi ʋ nanna boliŋ me yaa nyaaŋ sʋŋ daaŋ mana.

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Maŋ yaa wa keŋ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ, ama die ba ka bɩagɩ a gbaaŋ wa.”

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Nɩnɩŋ lele vuosi gie dɩ wone yada ta bɩ yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩɩsa Ŋmɩŋ, nɩ yaala n bemme nɩ jigiŋ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Nɩ yaala n yaa wa suguru aŋaŋ nɩ a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa bɩa? Nɩ yaa bʋa wa a keŋ giena.”

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Yisa die dɩ natɩ jɩmbɩakʋ die ka nyɩŋ bʋa wa ma, die bʋa wa dɩ ye a laafɩa lele womi.

ref: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
text: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa, “Bɩa wɩa tɩ ka bɩagɩ a yagɩ jɩmbɩakʋ?”

ref: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
text: Die Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mana die dɩ lagɩsɩna Galili mi, die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Ba nan nagɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa a yi vuosi nuusi me

ref: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
text: aŋ ba kʋʋ mɩŋ ama daraa ataa daraaŋ n nan hagɩ kumbu me.” Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba sʋgɩtɩ die dɩ paalɩ a chʋʋsɩ. Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ lampo tumiŋ wɩa

ref: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
text: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ gana Kapenɔmi, Ŋmɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ lampotuosiribe dɩ keŋ Piita jigiŋ a pɩasɩ dɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ wa tune Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ lampoke mɩŋ?”

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Ta Piita die dɩ baarɩ, wa, ʋ tune mɩŋ. Piita die dɩ gana a juu tigiri me die Yisa die woliŋ balɩ wɩaha, ta pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Simoni lalɩa fʋ yile? Ba mba tunene lampo a yɩa tɩŋgbaŋka gie naakpɩɩma ha? Tɩŋgbaŋka vuosi yaa chaanɩŋ?”

ref: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
text: Ta ʋ yiŋŋi a baarɩ “Chaanɩŋ.” Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ, “Dɩ mʋ mɩŋ, dɩ dagɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka vuosi ka mʋ dɩ ba tuŋ.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Ama tɩ kana ka yaala tɩ chʋʋsɩ ba sʋgɩtɩ wɩa, ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma a ga taaŋ kagɔntɩ ta datɩ zaasɩ dieke fʋ wolinne a yigi, fʋ yuori ka nʋaŋ nan ye ligire biŋ dieke dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a tuŋ manɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ ma; ta fʋ nagɩ ka a tuŋ be.”

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: Dɩ bɩ yiwo fʋ nimbiŋ dɩaŋ yinene fʋ tʋma tʋmbɩatɩ fʋ vʋarɩka a vigi taaŋ dama dɩ kpɩa fʋ baaŋ nan juu miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ ma aŋaŋ nimbibalɩmɩŋ a tɩaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yallɩ nine ale ta Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ fʋ a taaŋ boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ ma. Yii diekemba dɩ bana nandagɩrɩ (Luki

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Dɩ tɩŋ mʋ fʋ chɩgɩ wa fʋ chanchaaka zɔɔlɩŋ sɩba die n chɩgɩna fʋ zɔɔlɩŋ die wo gbaŋ gbaŋ.’

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Die wɩa vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ sɩba bʋabike gie, wʋnɩŋ ʋ balla ʋ yi vuokpɩɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: Vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a tuose bʋabike gie chanchaaŋ n saaŋ ma, mɩŋŋɩ tuose mɩŋ mɩŋ. Magɩsɩŋ wɩa (Maki

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: Ʋ bɩ ka yaala ʋ wʋŋ a yɩ ba, fʋ balɩ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ vuosi mana, dɩɩ yi ʋ ka bɩ wʋŋ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ vuosisi, nɩ va wa aŋ ʋ yi sɩba vuodieke dɩ kana ka jɩama Ŋmɩŋ yaa lampotuosiru. Chʋara aŋaŋ jadieke dɩ kana ka chʋarɩ wɩa

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: “Ballɩbɩ diekemba dɩ yine mɩŋ yada wa gie, vuodieke dɩ yi ba yi bɩaŋ, ba tɩŋ bɔbɩ nɩɩŋ a yi ʋ nyie ta nagɩ wa taaŋ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma ta nyaabʋ dɩ dii wo dɩ tɩŋ nan dɩ kpɩa.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: “Dɩ yiwo fʋ nuuŋ yaa fʋ nachɩgɩsɩŋ yinene fʋ yie tʋmbɩatɩ die nɩŋ fʋ gobike a vigi taaŋ. Dama dɩ kpɩa fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yallɩ nuubalɩmɩŋ yaa nachɩgɩbalɩmɩŋ a juu miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩ ma a tɩaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yallɩ nuusi mana aŋaŋ nachɩgɩsɩ mana ta Ŋmɩŋ nagɩ fʋ a vigi taaŋ boli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ ma.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: “Fʋ nɩmbʋa dɩ keŋ tʋŋ fʋ bɩaŋ, ga ʋ jigiŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ benne ʋ nyɩɩna ma a balɩ wa wudieke ʋ yine. Dɩɩ yi ʋ saagɩ ʋ tʋnna die, nɩ bɩ yiwo nɩmballɩ bɩbra die.

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa dɩ ka yaala ballɩbɩsɩ gie wʋnyɩ bɩa. Tɩ chanchaalɩŋ wʋbɩatɩ nagɩ chaala wɩa

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Ama ʋ a keŋ ka yaala ʋ wʋŋ a yɩ fʋ, fʋ nagɩ vuobalɩmɩŋ yaa vuosi bale a gʋtɩ fʋ ma aŋ nɩ ga ʋ jigiŋ, aŋ vuosi bale wo yaa bataa wa wʋŋ wɩarɩ ta yi fʋ siaratieliŋ.

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: “Wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, jadieke dɩ baa dɩ chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me die gbaŋ gbaŋ nan dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma; ta jadieke dɩ kana ka chʋarɩ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me die gbaŋ gbaŋ jaabʋ kaaŋ dɩ chʋarɩ arɩzanna ma.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “N bɩ bala nɩ, nɩnɩŋ nɩ vuosi bale dɩ yi nʋabalɩmɩŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me a jʋʋsɩ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jaaŋ, ʋ nan yi nɩ yaalala die a yɩ nɩ.

ref: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
text: Dama jigidieke mana vuosi bale yaa bataa dɩ lagɩsɩna n saaŋ ma, mi maŋ gʋtɩ a bie ba sʋŋ.” Yɔmʋ dieke dɩ kana ka nagɩ chaa nandagɩrɩ wɩa

ref: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
text: Die Piita die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, dɩɩ yi n nɩmbʋa dɩ yi mɩŋ bɩaŋ, bʋŋmɩa maŋ baa n nagɩ a chaa wa? Bʋyʋpɔyɩ?”

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: Dama Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba naaŋ wʋnyɩ dɩ wana ʋ tigiŋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ʋ bɩɩsɩ ba hamɩŋ ligire dɩ mʋna die.

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: Die ʋ piiline a bɩɩsa ba ba yaa daa wʋnyɩ a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, die ʋ dii wo ʋ hamɩŋ ligire tuse tuse pam.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Die ʋ kaaŋ dɩ bɩagɩ a tuŋ; naa chɩaŋ ma naaŋ wa die dɩ a baarɩ ba nagɩ wa aŋaŋ ʋ hɔgʋ aŋaŋ ʋ ballɩ aŋaŋ nyindiekemba ʋ yalla mana a daa a keŋ tuŋ hannɩ.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: Ama yɔmʋ wa die dɩ gbirigi naaŋ wa nɩŋŋa a jʋʋsɩ wa a baarɩ, ‘Dii suguru, n nan tuŋ fʋ jaaŋ mana.’

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Ta zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi naaŋ wa die ʋ nagɩ hannɩ a chaa wa, ta va ʋ ga.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: Ʋ chanchaaka die dɩ a gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa a jʋʋsɩ wa a baarɩ, ‘Dii suguru n nan tuŋ fʋ!’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: Ama ta die ʋ zeti ta baarɩ ba yigi wo a ga a yi dansarɩka ma a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ baaŋ nan tuŋ hannɩ.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Die ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba die dɩ yene die wo die ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ paalɩ chʋʋsɩ die ba ga balɩ ba naaŋ wa wudieke mana dɩ yine.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Naaŋ wa die dɩ wa wa ʋ juu; ʋ piili a zɩa wa a baarɩ dɩ, ‘Fʋnɩŋ vuobɩakʋ gie die n nagɩ hannɩ dieke fʋ dinene a chaa fʋ mɩŋ dama die jʋʋsɩ mɩŋ mɩŋ.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Die sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ dɩ paala yallɩ naaŋ wa ʋ nagɩ daa wa a yi dansarɩka sʋŋ dɩ ʋ dii wahala a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke ʋ baaŋ nan tuŋ hannɩ mana.”

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Nandagɩrɩ gie kʋaŋ chaaŋ die Yisa dɩ balɩ dɩ, “Dɩɩ yi fʋ ka nagɩ a chaa fʋ chanchaalɩŋ aŋaŋ fʋ sʋŋ mana die gbaŋ gbaŋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa dɩ nan yi fʋ.”

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ balla wɩaha a kpatɩ naa, ʋ nyɩŋ Galili a ga Judia tɩŋgbaŋ dieke dɩ benne Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ chaakʋ.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Dama wɩa pam chɩaŋ ma dembisi bataŋ dɩ ka faara hɔɔŋ; bataŋ ba ŋaaŋ mɩɩrɩ ba ta ba ka yi dembisi; bataŋ dɩaŋ vuota yine be die; ta bataŋ dɩaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa ba zete; ama vuodieke dɩ nan bɩagɩ a tuo dagɩkʋ gie, ʋ tuoke vɩɩnɩŋ.” Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩna a tuo ballɩbɩsɩ dene (Maki

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Ta kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ die ʋ gbaaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ yʋagɩna mi.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Ta Farasisi bataŋ dɩ a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a yaala ba magɩsɩ wa, ta pɩasɩ wa, “Tɩ mɩraha yɩ sieŋ dɩ dembiŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ ta ʋ ka yi wɩɩŋ mɩŋ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba, “Nɩ ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ dɩ bala die wo? Dɩ ‘Piiliku me, Ŋmɩŋ die naanna vuota, dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ,

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: ta baarɩ, Naa chɩaŋ ma dembiŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ nan va ba chʋalɩŋ aŋaŋ ba nɩɩlɩŋ ta faarɩ taŋ ta ba vuosi bale wo lagɩŋ a yi nyɩŋgbaŋ balɩmɩŋ.’

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa ba ka bɩ yi vuosi bale, ba wa yiwo vuobalɩmɩŋ. Die wɩa jadieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩna a lagɩŋ taŋ, vuota ka mʋ ʋ puoke.”

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Die Farasisi die dɩ pɩasɩ wa, “Bɩa wɩa Mosisi die dɩ yɩ mɩra dɩ dembiŋ dɩ a keŋ a baa ʋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ sie ʋ woliŋ a yɩ wa gbanɩŋ a baarɩ dɩ ʋ ka bɩ yaala wa?”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩ tɩba dɩ hagɩrɩna wɩa die Mosisi die dɩ yɩ nɩ sieŋ dɩ nɩ nan bɩagɩ a zeti nɩ hɔɔŋ. Ama piiliku me da die ka sɩɩ.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, vuodieke mana dɩ keŋ zeti ʋ hɔgʋ da daakpana tʋʋma wɩa ta faarɩ hɔgʋ gaaŋ ʋ yiwo hɔgʋkpana tʋʋma; ).”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, “Da vuoŋ mana baaŋ bɩagɩ a tuo dagɩkʋ gie sie vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna ba sieŋ dɩ ba tuohe wo.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Die vuosi bataŋ die dɩ chii ba ballɩ a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ba ma ta jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ a yɩ ba ama die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nata ba.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Saŋka mi dalʋakɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ, lalɩa maŋ nan yi ta ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ta daa wa die dɩ pɩasɩ Yisa, “Mɩrɩ bɩaŋtɩ?” Ta ʋ balɩ wa dɩ, “Da keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ; da keŋ a yaalɩ vuoŋ hɔgʋ; da keŋ a gaarɩ; da keŋ a yi ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ siaratieŋ;

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: Die ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi diisi ba ma ta die chʋŋ ga. Dalʋakɩ ligiretieŋ wɩa (Maki

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, “Bɩa wɩa fʋ pɩasa mɩŋ wʋvɩɩna wɩa? Vuobalɩmɩŋ nyɩɩna ma vɩɩŋ na; ama fʋ yaala fʋ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ fʋ dɩa mɩraha.”

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Ta Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba, “Nɩ vaa ballɩbɩsɩsɩ keŋ n jigiŋ ta nɩ da kagɩ ba sieŋ dama ba chanchaalɩŋ sɩna Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ.”

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Ama vuosi pam die dɩ wolinne a bie nɩŋŋa nan wɩarɩ kʋaŋ ta vuosi pam die dɩ wɩarɩna kʋaŋ nan tɩaŋ nɩŋŋa.”

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Yɩma fʋ chʋa aŋaŋ fʋ naa jɩlɩma; ta chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ chone fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die wo.”

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Ta dalʋakɩrɩ die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, “N wɔŋ a dɩɩ mɩraha gie mana mɩŋ. Wʋbɩa bɩna a tɩalɩ dɩ n yi a gʋtɩ?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Ta Yisa die dɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, “Fʋ yaala fʋ bɩrɩŋ vuovɩɩnɩŋ wusie ga a ga nagɩ fʋ nyinti mana a daa ta nagɩ ligirehe a yɩ zɔɔlɩntieliŋ ta fʋ bɩrɩŋ ligiretieŋ arɩzanna ma, ta fʋ keŋ a dɩa n kʋaŋ.”

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Die dalʋakɩrɩ dɩ wʋnna naa wa ʋ sʋŋ dɩ chʋʋsɩ ʋ chʋŋ ga, dama die ʋ faasɩ yiwo nyintitieŋ.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Naa chɩaŋ ma Yisa die dɩ a balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, “Wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, dɩ nan dɩ tʋa dɩ ligiretieŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: N bala nɩ bɩbra, dɩ nan yi mɔlɩ dɩ nyɔgɩnɩ daagɩ garɩyɩɩŋ vɔrɩŋ ma a nyɩŋ a tɩaŋ ligiretieŋ dɩ baaŋ nan juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna naa die dɩ paalɩ a yi be mamachi die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, “Mɩnɩa baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ?”

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Die Yisa dɩ kɔtɩ ʋ sikpeŋ a daansɩ ba ta die balɩ yɩ ba dɩ, “Vuota nɩŋ jigiŋ dɩ tʋa mɩŋ, ama Ŋmɩŋ nɩŋ nan bɩagɩ a yi jaaŋ mana.”

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Die Piita die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, “Tɩnɩŋ nɩŋ tɩ va wa tɩ nyinti mana ta keŋ dɩ fʋ. Bɩa yine tɩ nyʋarɩ?”

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, saŋŋa dieke manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ keŋ kalɩ n naara gbaŋtɩ ma aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ dʋnɩa haalɩkʋ ma, nɩnɩŋ die dɩ yine n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩaŋ nan dɩ kalɩ naara gbaŋtɩ baŋ aŋaŋ ale mi a dii Izara buuriŋ baŋ aŋaŋ ale wo sarɩya.

ref: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
text: Ta vuodieke mana dɩ vana ʋ tigiŋ yaa ʋ nɩmballɩ yaa ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ yaa ʋ ballɩ yaa ʋ kʋatɩ n wɩa, ʋ nan daansɩ ye a chanchaaŋ kɔbɩga kɔbɩga, ta bɩ ye miivoli dieke dɩ wone kpatɩŋ.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: “Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba tigiŋ tieŋ dieke dɩ nyɩnna sʋkʋleeliŋ a yaala vuosi dɩ ʋ nagɩ aŋ ba keŋ tʋŋ ʋ kʋaŋ ma.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ba die dɩ a balɩ a zieŋ dɩ ʋ nan tuŋ be dɩnda daarɩ mi tune; die ba ga piili kpɩaŋ.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: Die dɩ wa tʋga hawa awayɩ saŋŋa, tigiŋ tieŋ wo dɩ bɩ nyɩŋ a ga a ga ye vuosi bataŋ ba zene nyʋʋŋ jigiŋ ta ka yie wɩɩŋ mana.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Ta ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba, ‘Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ga a ga tʋŋ tʋʋma n kʋakʋ ma, ta n nan tuŋ nɩ tuŋ dieke dɩ mʋna.’

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
text: Die wɩa die ba ga. Die dɩ tʋgɩna agbaa saŋŋa die ʋ bɩ yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ, ta agbaa die dɩ tɩanna hawasɩ ataa kʋaŋ chaaŋ, die ʋ bɩ yi die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Die dɩ wa tʋga jɩŋmɩŋ , die ʋ bɩ a ga nyʋʋkʋ chaaŋ a ye dembisi bataŋ dɩ zene mi ta ka tʋma tʋʋmɩŋ mana. Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, ‘Bɩa nɩ zie giena yɔrɩ damʋʋmɩŋ ta ka yie wɩɩŋ?’

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, ‘Vuoŋ ka keŋ nagɩ tɩ dɩ tɩ ga tʋŋ ʋ tʋʋma.’ Die wɩa ʋ balɩ ba, ‘Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ga a ga tʋŋ tʋʋma n kʋakʋ ma.’

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Die ŋmɩŋ dɩ nanna, tigiritieŋ wo dɩ wa ʋ tʋntʋntɩba jakʋʋŋ dɩ, ‘Ga wa tʋntʋntɩba a tuŋ be ba tune, piili a nyɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kenne kʋaka a ga tʋgɩ vuodiekemba dɩ wolinne wo.’

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Ta ʋ tʋntʋntʋ jakʋʋrɩ die dɩ tuŋ vuodiekemba dɩ wɩarɩna kʋaka a keŋ wo dɩnda daarɩ mi tune.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Die ba tuo ba ligirehe a kpatɩ die ba piili a vʋʋna ta galɩma tigiritieŋ wo,

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: a baarɩ, ‘Vuosi gie wɩarɩ wa kʋaŋ a keŋ kʋakʋ, agbaa kʋaŋ hawasɩ anʋ saŋŋa ta kʋa hawa balɩmɩŋ, ama tɩnɩŋ tɩ dine wahala a ga a gbalɩgɩ a nyɩŋ sʋkʋʋŋ mana, ta ŋmɩnnɩ dɩ nɩgɩ tɩ pam, ama tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ banɩŋ dɩ lagɩsɩ a tuo tuŋ balɩmɩŋ.’

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: “Die tigiritieŋ wo dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba wʋnyɩ, ‘N zʋa, wʋmma, n ka nyɩrɩ nɩ, dama tɩ tɩŋ balɩ zie mɩŋ nɩ saagɩ dɩnda daarɩ mi tune.

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Die wɩa nagɩ fʋ tʋaŋ a ga tigiŋ. Manɩŋ n yaala n tuŋ wo nɩ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ kenne kʋaka bʋnyɩ.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: N wo sieŋ dɩ n nagɩ n ligirehe a yi wudieke n yaalala? Yaa wa sʋgɩbɩaŋ dama n kana ka mʋma wɩa?’ ”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ a kpatɩ wa kʋaŋ chaaŋ ta ʋ balɩ ba dɩ, “Die wɩa vuodiekemba dɩ benne kʋaŋ nan dɩ bie nɩŋŋa ta vuodiekemba dɩ benne nɩŋŋa wɩarɩ kʋaŋ.” Yisa die dɩ bɩna balɩ ʋ kuŋ wɩa bʋtaa ma dene (Maki

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Yisa die dɩ zie ta wa ba a keŋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Bɩa nɩ yaala n yi a yɩ nɩ?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Ta zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi Yisa, ta ʋ gbi gbi ba ninehe, die ba ninehe die dɩ yuori yuori, ta ba dɩ ʋ kʋaŋ.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Ama kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ natɩ ba dɩ ba tarɩ watɩ, ama ta die ba wa a faasɩ a keese, “Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, Davidi haagɩŋ, chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.”

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Die Yisa die dɩ jʋalɩna a gara Jerusalemi, die ʋ wa ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo a nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ a die a balɩ a yɩ ba.

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: ta nagɩ mɩŋ a yi vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka yi Juu vuosi nuusi me aŋ ba vʋarɩ mɩŋ fala, ta nɩgɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ kpaasɩŋ ta kpaasɩ mɩŋ a diisi dagarɩkʋ ma, aŋ n kpi ama a daraa ataa ma n nan hagɩ kuŋ me.” Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn nuŋ die dɩ keŋ jʋʋsɩ Yisa jakʋʋŋ wɩa (Maki

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Womi Zebedi hɔgʋ die dɩ chii ʋ ballɩ bale a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ, ta gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa ta jʋʋsɩ wa suŋŋiŋ.

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Bɩa fʋ yaala?” Die ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ wa, “Yɩ mɩŋ nʋaŋ dɩ n ballɩ gie wʋnyɩ nan daansɩ kalɩ fʋ nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ fʋ nuugalɩ chaaŋ saŋŋa dieke fʋ keŋ dii fʋ naarɩ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
text: Ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩ zɩ nɩ jʋʋsɩnana jadieke. Nɩ nan bɩagɩ a chii chiiti dieke n balla n chii wo?” Die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, “Wa, tɩ nan bɩagɩ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Wusie, nɩ nan chii chiiti dieke n bala n chii wo, ama n wo sieŋ dɩ n vʋarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ mʋna ba kala n nuudiigiŋ aŋaŋ n nuugalɩ chaaŋ. Jigehe gie zie gbarɩ wa vuodiekemba n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ vʋarɩna dɩ ba kala mi.”

ref: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
text: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩ tɩalɩkaha die dɩ wʋnna naa, die ba jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yi ba bale wo.

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa Yisa die dɩ wa ba mana a lagɩsɩ taŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ ba, “Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ sɩba a baarɩ vuodiekemba dɩ daansɩnana vuosi tɩŋgbaŋka gie me ŋaaŋ daga wa ba gbaŋ ta nyɩŋkʋraha dɩaŋ ŋaaŋ pɔgɩlɩ wa ba vuosi aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna.

ref: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
text: Ama nɩnɩŋ die wo, nɩ sʋnsʋŋ vuodieke dɩ yaala nyɩŋkʋrɩtɩ, sie ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ wabʋ a tʋma a yɩma vuoŋ mana.

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
text: Vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ bɩ yaala ʋ bemme nɩŋŋa, sie ʋ nagɩ ʋ gbaŋ a bɩrɩŋ nɩ mana yɔmʋ,

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: sɩba manɩŋ vuota Bʋa dɩ sɩna die wo, dama n ka keŋye dɩ vuosi tʋma a yɩma mɩŋ ama n keŋye dɩ n tʋŋ yɩ vuosi, ta bɩ kpi a yi vuosi pam ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ.” Yisa die dɩ yuorine yɩɩsɩ bale nine dene (Maki

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ nyɩnana Jeriko me, die kpɩkpaaŋ die dɩ dɩ Yisa kʋaŋ.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Ta yɩɩsɩ bale die dɩ kalɩ sieku nʋaŋ a wʋŋ dɩ Yisa keŋ tɩanna mɩŋ, die wɩa die ba piili a keese a baarɩ, “Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, naaŋ Davidi haagɩŋ chɩgɩ tɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.”

ref: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
text: Ba yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, “Tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ, tɩ yaala fʋ yuori wo tɩ nine.”

ref: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
text: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Lalɩa nɩ yile yaa gamma wɩarɩ gie? Daa wʋnyɩ die yalla ballɩ bale, ta die balɩ a yɩ jakʋʋrɩ dɩ, ‘N bʋa, ga kʋakʋ ma a tʋŋ tʋʋma jinne.’

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Ama tʋntʋntɩba die dɩ yene ʋ bʋa wa die ba balɩ yɩ tamba, ‘Ʋ faarɩ diiru wo wʋnna, nɩ vaa tɩ kʋʋ wa ta nyinti bɩrɩŋ tɩ sɩɩtɩ!’

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ pɩasɩ nɩ wɩɩŋ, nɩ balɩ wa, nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ yaalala ka, ta ʋ nan vaa nɩ yaa ha keŋ.”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Wɩaha gie mana die keŋ yi mɩŋ sɩba die Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vana ʋ naazʋalɩba wʋnyɩ die dɩ balɩna die wo dɩ

ref: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
text: “Nɩ balɩ Zayɔni tɩka vuosi dɩ, ‘Ye, nɩ naaŋ wa kieŋ nɩ jigiŋ. Ʋ yiwo vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna ʋ gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ, ta jʋala boŋ dieke dɩ yi bonpɔlɩ.’ ”

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga yi wudieke Yisa die dɩ balla ba dɩ ba yi wo.

ref: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
text: Die ba yaa bonɩɩkʋ aŋaŋ ka bʋa a keŋ, a nagɩ ba nyinyeeke a diisi a kʋasɩ ta Yisa dɩ jʋalɩ.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Die Yisa die dɩ gana a ga juu Jerusalemi, tɩka vuosi mana die dɩ hagɩ a zie a pɩasa taŋ dɩ, “Mɩnɩa wʋnna?”

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
text: Ama die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ die ba yene ʋ yinene mamachi wɩaha ta bɩ wʋŋ ballɩbɩsɩsɩ dɩ natɩnana Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma a baarɩ, “Hozaana, nɩ bɩrɩma Davidi haagɩrɩ gie die wo!”

ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Farasisi die dɩ wʋnna nandagaha die ba mɩŋŋɩ sɩba banɩŋ ba wɩa Yisa die taaŋ nandagɩrɩ.

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: ta die a balɩ ba dɩ, “Dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ wʋnɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ, ‘N tigiri gie nan dɩ wasa Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ jigiŋ’ ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ nagɩ ka a bɩrɩŋ gaarɩŋ jigiŋ.”

ref: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
text: Dɩɩ yi nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada nɩ nan ye jadieke mana nɩ jʋʋsɩnana a yaala.” Vuosi bataŋ die dɩ pɩasɩna Yisa yaa gamma ʋ yiko ke wɩa dene (Maki

ref: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
text: Die ba pɩasɩ wa, “Wʋŋ wudieke ba balala wa mɩŋ?” Ta ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Wa, ama dɩ maagɩya Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ dɩ, ‘Fʋ yine ballɩbɩsɩsɩ aŋaŋ ballɩnyaalɩsɩ dɩ bɩra fʋ vɩɩnɩŋ.’ Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ wɩaha gie Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ?”

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Yisa die dɩ va ba, ta nyɩŋ tɩka sʋŋ a ga Betani, die a dʋa mi tʋŋ dɩ vʋŋ. Yisa die dɩ kana yɩ kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩka dene (Maki

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Ta Yisa dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “N nan pɩasɩ nɩ wʋbalɩmɩŋ, dɩɩ yi nɩ bɩagɩ a balɩ mɩŋ ka chɩaŋ, n nan balɩ nɩ yiko dieke a yalla a yie wɩaha gie:

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Tʋŋ die dɩ vʋʋna sʋkʋleeliŋ die ʋ yiŋŋi a gara tɩka sʋŋ, die kɔŋ die dɩ yallɩ wa.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, dɩɩ yi nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ yada ta ka chɩɩlɩya nɩ nan bɩagɩ a yi wudieke n yine yɩ kɩnkamɩŋ tɩɩka gie; daa genke nyɩɩna ma, nɩ nan bɩagɩ a balɩ yɩ kunkogiri gie dɩ, vʋʋ a ga nan mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma aŋ ka seŋ yi.

ref: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
text: Die wɩa die ba yiŋŋi a balɩ Yisa dɩ, “Tɩ ka sɩba.” Die ʋ balɩ yɩ ba, “Die nɩŋ n kaaŋ balɩ nɩ vuodieke dɩ yɩna mɩŋ yiko maŋ yaa yie tʋʋmaha gie.” Daa wʋnyɩ ballɩ bale nandagɩrɩ wɩa

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a balɩ ba, “Nɩ bɩ wʋŋ nandagɩŋ kaanɩ bɩbra. Tigiŋ tieŋ wʋnyɩ die benne a sʋʋ tɩɩ diekemba dɩ baaŋ nan nyɩŋ nyɩŋnyɩŋka ʋ kʋaŋ ma, a nagɩ daatɩ a kpie giliŋke, ta die a wʋnsɩ vɔrɩŋ, jigidieke ba baaŋ dɩ ŋmʋa nyɩŋnyɩŋka nyaabʋ a yie, ta kpii vɩɩŋ mi. Ta nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ tʋntʋntɩba nuusi me dɩ ba daansɩma. Dɩɩ yi a keŋ ŋmɩntɩ dɩ ba kpaa a yɩ wa ta kpaa ba tʋaŋ. Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ die ʋ dɩa a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ma, ta die daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋra die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Bɩa yiko fʋ yaa yie wɩaha gie? Mɩnɩa yɩna fʋ yiko ke gie?”

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ, Ŋmɩŋ die yɩna wa yiko yaa vuota?” Ba piili a nɩga nɩnhagɩrɩŋ dɩ, “Bɩa tɩ baa tɩ balɩ? Tɩ keŋ a baarɩ ‘Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ’, ʋ nan yiŋŋi a balɩ tɩ dɩ, ‘Ta bɩa wɩa nɩ ka yi wo yada?’

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Ama tɩ keŋ baarɩ ka nyɩŋ vuota jigiŋ, tɩ chɩga ŋmaamɩŋ aŋaŋ wudieke kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ baaŋ yi, dama vuoŋ mana die yi yada dɩ Jɔɔn seŋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.”

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: Bʋa wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, ‘N kaaŋ ga’ ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ tarɩgɩ ʋ sʋŋanyile ta ga kʋakʋ.

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: Die chɔɔŋ wa dɩ ga bʋabike jigiŋ a balɩ die gbaŋ gbaŋ a yɩ wa, ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ dɩ, ‘N chʋa n nan ga’, ama die ʋ ka gaya.”

ref: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
text: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ gie a kpatɩ die ʋ pɩasɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ, “Ballɩ bale wo gie, ba jabɩa yine ʋ chɔɔŋ dɩ yaalala dene?” Die ba yiŋŋi a baarɩ, “Jakʋʋrɩ.” Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, lampotuosiribe aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dʋaga hɔgʋkpana yaa daakpana nan woliŋ a juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma nɩ ma.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Dama Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die keŋ nɩ jigiŋ a dagɩ nɩ sievɩɩŋkʋ ama die nɩ ka yi wo yada, ama lampotuosiribe aŋaŋ hɔgʋkpana aŋaŋ daakpana die dɩ yi wo yada. Die nɩ yene wɩaha gie mana kʋaŋ chaaŋ, nɩ ka tarɩgɩ nɩ sʋŋanyile ta yi wo yada.” Tʋntʋntɩba nandagɩrɩ (Maki

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Die nyɩnyɩŋkaha kpaala saŋŋa die dɩ kenne tʋgɩ die ʋ tʋŋ ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ba ga tʋntʋntɩba jigiŋ a ga tuo ʋ tʋaŋ.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: Ama ta tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yigi ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ a nɩgɩ wʋnyɩ pam ta kʋʋ wʋnyɩ ta taa wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ aŋaŋ tana.

ref: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
text: Daa wa dɩ bɩ tʋŋ yɔŋɩsɩ bataŋ a tɩaŋ vuodiekemba ʋ wolinne a tʋŋ wa, ta tʋntʋntɩba dɩ yi be die gbaŋ gbaŋ.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: Kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ tʋŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ Bʋadembiŋ. A baarɩ, ‘N sɩba baarɩ ba nan yɩ n bʋa wa jɩlɩma.’

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Ta die ba yigi wo a nagɩ wa a nyɩŋ kʋakʋ lʋgɩŋ a kʋʋ wa.”

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ gie a kpatɩ die ʋ pɩasɩ ba, “Die wɩa kʋakʋ tieŋ dɩ keŋ keŋ bɩa ʋ baa ʋ yi tʋntʋntɩba mi?”

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa dɩ, “Ʋ nan kʋʋ banɩŋ tʋntʋmbɩatɩ tielibe ta nagɩ kʋakʋ a yɩ vuogaasɩ, vuodiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan yɩ wa ʋ nyɩŋnyɩŋkaha tʋaŋ a kpaala saŋŋa.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Yisa dɩ balɩ yɩ ba, “Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ die dɩ maagɩna die wo? Dɩ maagɩya dɩ ‘Taŋ dieke mɩmɩɩrɩba die dɩ zetine ta baarɩ ba ka yaala ka wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ yi gɔŋgɔŋyʋgɩrɩŋ taŋ vɩɩnɩŋ, Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ yine die ta a faasɩ yi tɩ mamachi!’ ”

ref: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
text: Die wɩa Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba, “Wusie maŋ balɩ nɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan gbatɩ ʋ naarɩ a nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ ta nagɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ baaŋ nan vaa ʋ dii ka nyʋarɩ.

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: Die ba yaala ba yigi wo, ama die ba chɩga kpɩkpaakʋ ŋmaamɩŋ dama vuosi die yi yada a baarɩ dɩ Yisa yiwo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Die Farasisi vuosi die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga a lagɩsɩ tamba a yaala sieti dɩ ba yigi Yisa aŋaŋ wʋpɩasɩka.

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: Die a baarɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ, Mosisi die balɩ dɩ dembiŋ dɩ keŋ faarɩ hɔgʋ ta kpi ta wo ballɩ, sie ʋ nɩmbʋa faarɩ kpihɔgʋ wa a mɩɩrɩ ballɩ a yɩ vuodieke dɩ kpine wo.

ref: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
text: Yisa die dɩ bɩ nagɩ nandaga a balɩ wɩa a yɩ vuosisi dɩ,

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: “Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba naaŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ wʋnsɩna hɔgʋhaalʋ banwʋla dɩ ʋ yɩ ʋ bʋa.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ta die tʋŋ ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ba ga balɩ vuodiekemba ʋ wone a posi wo dɩ ba keŋ banwʋlaka, ama die ba ka yaala ba keŋ.

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: Die ʋ bɩ tʋŋ yɔŋɩsɩ bataŋ ta balɩ ba dɩ, ‘Nɩ gamma a ga balɩ vuodiekemba die n wone a posi wo dɩ chaantɩ nyindiike yi siri mɩŋ; n wɔŋ kʋʋ narapɔlɩka aŋaŋ naara narɩka ta a mana yi wo siri; die wɩa nɩ keŋ a keŋ dii banwʋlaka chaantɩ!’

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: Ama die ʋ posine vuodiekemba ba die ka tɔgɩŋ wa ta die ga ba tʋʋma jige; wʋnyɩ dɩ ga ʋ kʋaŋ, ta wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ dɩ ga ʋ nyinti daala jigiŋ nyʋʋŋ ma,

ref: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
text: ta bataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ yigi ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ a nɩgɩ nɩgɩ ba ta kʋʋ ba.

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: Die naaŋ wa dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ pam; ʋ tʋŋ ʋ sojasi ba ga kʋʋ vuokʋʋlaha ta jʋʋ ba tɩka boliŋ.

ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: A kʋaŋ chaaŋ die naaŋ wa dɩ wa ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ a balɩ ba dɩ, ‘N hɔgʋhaalʋ banwʋlaka chaantɩ yi siri mɩŋ, ama vuodiekemba n wone a posi wo ka mʋ dɩ ba keŋ.

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Die wɩa nɩ wa gamma tɩka sʋŋ siekpɩɩma ma, a wa vuodieke mana nɩ baaŋ nan tuoli dɩ ba keŋ a dii laalɩkʋ chaantɩ.’

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: “Ta Naaŋ wa die dɩ keŋ dɩ ʋ daansɩ ye vuodiekemba dɩ kenne banwʋlaka, die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ dɩ benne vuosi sʋnsʋŋ ta ka yeegi banwʋla nyiŋyeeke.

ref: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
text: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ die dɩ yi ʋ nɩmbʋa die dɩ diisine bule wo a yaa ga tʋgɩ vuodieke die dɩ diisine bʋtaa wa, a yaa die a ga kpatɩ dembisi bayʋpɔyɩ wa mana.

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Die ba tʋŋ ba kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataŋ aŋaŋ Herodi vuosi bataŋ Yisa jigiŋ. Die ba pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ, tɩ sɩba a baarɩ bala wusie, ta daga vuosi Ŋmɩŋ sieti aŋaŋ wusie, ta ka tɔgɩma vuosi, dama fʋ ka tɔgɩma vuota dɩ sɩna die.

ref: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
text: Nɩ nagɩ ligiribiŋ dieke nɩ balla nɩ nagɩ a tuŋ lampoke a dagɩ mɩŋ.” Die ba yaa ligire bini a keŋ yɩ wa,

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Die ba yiŋŋi baarɩ, “Naaŋ Siiza.” Die chɩaŋ ma die Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Die nɩŋ nɩ tuŋ a yɩ naaŋ Siiza jadieke dɩ yine ʋ sɩɩtɩ ta tuŋ yɩ Ŋmɩŋ jadieke dɩ yine Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ.”

ref: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
text: Die ba wʋnna naa wa die dɩ yi be mamachi, die ba va wa ta ga. Kunti hagɩŋ wɩa (Maki

ref: At last the woman died.
text: Ka kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ hɔgʋ wa dɩaŋ die dɩ kpi.

ref: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
text: Mosisi mɩraha mana aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dagɩtɩ mana diisi wo mɩraha ale wo gie me.” Mɩnɩa bʋa yine Masia wa (Maki

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Dɩnɩŋ daarɩ mi gbaŋ gbaŋ die Sadusi vuosi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Nɩ baya mɩŋ, dama nɩ ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ, ta bɩ ka sɩba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yalla hagɩrɩŋ dene.

ref: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
text: Dama kunti dɩ keŋ hagɩ kuŋ me ba nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba malakasi dɩ benne arɩzanna ma, ta hɔgʋ faarɩŋ aŋaŋ dembiŋ yallɩŋ kaaŋ dɩ beri.

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
text: Yaa gamma kunti hagɩŋ ma nɩ ye ka karɩŋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ bala a yɩ nɩ wa ʋ gbaŋkʋ ma?

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
text: Dɩ ‘Manɩŋ n yine Ŋmɩŋ dieke Abarahami aŋaŋ Aziki aŋaŋ Jakobu dɩ jɩannana.’ Vuosi gie die kpiye mɩŋ ama ba ye ko bie ba mɩsɩ ma dama Ŋmɩŋ ka yi kunti Ŋmɩŋ ama vuodiekemba dɩ benne ba mɩsɩ ma?”

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Die kpɩkpaakʋ dɩ wʋnna naa, die dɩ yi be mamachi aŋaŋ ʋ dagɩkʋ. Mɩrɩ dieke dɩ tɩanna mɩraha mana wɩa (Maki

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
text: Die Farasisi dɩ wʋnna dɩ Yisa nyɩŋbalɩkaha die nyaŋŋɩ Sadusisi die ba lagɩsɩ tamba a keŋ.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: “Dɩdagɩrʋ, mɩrɩ bɩa tɩanna mɩraha mana?”

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Genke yine mɩrɩ dieke dɩ benne nɩŋŋa ta bɩ tɩaŋ mɩraha mana.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Ta jadieke dɩaŋ dɩ diisine bule wo dɩaŋ bɩ yiwo nɩŋŋmɩna a gʋtɩ, ‘Chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ.’

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Saŋŋa dieke ta Farasisi dɩ lagɩsɩna tamba die Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ,

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: ‘Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ die bala n Yɔmʋtieŋ; Kala n nuudiigiŋ chaaŋ, a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ nan nagɩ fʋ dataasɩ a yi fʋ nuusi me.’

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Bɩa nɩ yile yaa gamma Ŋmɩŋ vuovʋarɩkɩrɩ Masia wa wɩa? Mɩnɩa bʋa yine wo?” Ba yiŋŋi balɩ wa “Ʋ yiwo Davidi haagɩŋ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Die Yisa dɩ pɩasɩ ba, “Ta bɩa yine saŋŋa dieke Davidi die dɩ suuline aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ die dɩ wasa wa dɩ, n ‘Yɔmʋtieŋ?’ Ta baarɩ

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Dɩɩ yi ta Davidi die dɩ wasa Masia wa dɩ ‘N Yɔmʋtieŋ’, dɩ bɩ baa dɩ yiwo lalɩa Masia wa bɩ yi Davidi haagɩŋ?”

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Die ba wʋnyɩ mana die ka bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ wɩɩŋ, die wɩa a nyɩŋ dɩnda daarɩ mi yaa gamma vuoŋ die ka bɩ yaa sikimiŋ a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a pɩasɩ Yisa wʋpɩasɩka.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Ye, Ŋmɩŋ va nɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ta ka zie yɔrɩ.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: “Nchʋanaawee, Jerusalemi vuosi, nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kʋna Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba, ta bɩ taa vuodiekemba ʋ tʋnna nɩ jigiŋ wo tana, bʋŋmɩa maŋ mɩa, a yaala dɩ n lagɩsɩ nɩ bʋnyɩ sɩba kpɩanɩɩŋ dɩ ŋaana lagɩsɩ ka ballɩ ka chobiti chɩaŋ ta nɩ ka saaga.

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Die wɩa dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ huu kaabɩkʋ koriku, dɩ dagɩ sɩba ʋ huu kanɩŋ aŋaŋ jadieke mana dɩ diisine ka ma.

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ kpɩkpaakʋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ;

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: “Mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi yine vuodiekemba ba yɩna sieŋ dɩ ba yiŋŋi balɩma Mosisi mɩraha chɩasɩ.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Die chɩaŋ ma dɩ vɩɩna aŋ nɩ saagɩ ta dɩa ba dagɩna nɩ die mana, ama nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ yime wudieke ba yinene, dama ba ka dɩ wudieke ba balala wa.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Ba ŋaaŋ vaa wa vuosi chii chiidʋnsa, chii dieke dɩ dʋnsɩna a yɩa ba, ama ba ka saaga a suŋŋe be aŋ ba chii chiititi.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Wudieke mana ba yinene, ba yie mɩŋ dɩ vuota ye be. Ye, ba nagɩna Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ maagɩna bɩta gbanbisi me a bɔbɩ yi ba die aŋaŋ ba nuusi me ta a faasɩ dala dala die; bɩ ye ba nyiŋyeeki wagɩtɩ kʋaŋ nʋa dɩ wagɩrɩna ta faasɩ teeli die.

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Dɩɩ yi ba dʋʋga jige ba ŋaaŋ yaala ba kala sikpeŋkpɩɩma tieliŋ jigikasɩka; ta ba keŋ ga Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone jigiŋ, ba ŋaaŋ yaala ba kala vuokpɩɩma jigikasɩka;

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: ta bɩ yaala ba waasɩma ba aŋaŋ jɩlɩma nyʋgɩtɩ jige ta wasɩma ba ‘Dɩdagɩrʋ.’

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩŋ da keŋ yi ba wasɩma nɩ ‘Dɩdagɩrʋ’ dama nɩ mana yiwo nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ taŋ ta yaa nɩ Dɩdagɩrʋ balɩmɩŋ.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Ta nɩ daa keŋ wa vuoŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie me ‘Chɔɔŋ’ dama nɩ yaa wa Chɔɔŋ balɩmɩŋ; wʋnɩŋ ʋ yiwo Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Nɩ daa bɩ vaa vuosi wa nɩ ba ‘Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba’ dama nɩ yaa wa nɩ Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩ balɩmɩŋ wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Yisa Masia.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: “Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba yine gɩgaantɩba, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ gasa mʋgɩkpɩɩma ta chʋŋ gara tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ ta yaala vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan tarɩgɩ ʋ beriŋ a dɩa nɩ kʋaŋ, dɩɩ yi nɩ keŋ ye vuoŋ, nɩ ŋaaŋ yiwo ʋ tʋmbɩatɩ dieke dɩ yalla wa a gara Sitaani boliŋ me bɩ gʋtɩ die bule sɩba nɩnɩŋ.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: “Nɩnɩŋ yɩɩsɩ dɩdatɩrɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma! Nɩ daga vuosi dɩ, ‘Dɩɩ yi ta vuoŋ dɩ huu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ, dɩ ka yi nɩŋŋmɩna, ama dɩɩ yi ʋ keŋ huu ligire dieke dɩ benne juoku me wo sie ʋ yi dene.’

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Nɩnɩŋ yɩɩsɩ aŋaŋ gaantɩŋ, nyinti ale wo gie a jabɩa yine nɩŋŋmɩna, ligirehe yaa Ŋmɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ die dɩ yine ligirehe dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Ta nɩ bɩ daga dɩ, ‘Dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ huu kaabɩŋ koriŋ, ka ka yi nɩŋŋmɩna, ama ʋ keŋ huu kaabɩŋ dɔŋ dɩ diisine koriku me wo, sie ʋ yi dene gbaŋ gbaŋ.’

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Nɩ seŋ yiwo yɩɩsɩ wusie. Nyinti ale wa a jabɩa yine nɩŋŋmɩna, kaabɩŋ dɔŋkʋ yaa koriku dɩ yine kaabɩkʋ dɔnka die dɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ sɩɩtɩ?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Ta dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ huu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ, dɩ dagɩya sɩba ʋ huu kanɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ benne ka ma.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Dɩɩ bɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ huu arɩzanna, ʋ huu wa Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ gbaŋtɩtɩ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ kalala ka ma.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: “Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba die yine gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Nɩ naga nanjʋʋsɩ aŋaŋ bʋŋʋ aŋaŋ neeri aŋaŋ nyinti a taŋ baŋ sʋŋ a nagɩ kaanɩ a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ, ama ta va jadieke dɩ yine nɩŋŋmɩna ʋ mɩraha ma; sɩba wusie sarɩya diile, aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩŋ chɩgɩla aŋaŋ yada yiile. Genhe die mʋna nɩ yime, ta da va hanɩŋ ba yiile.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Nɩnɩŋ yɩɩsɩ diekemba dɩ datɩnana nɩ chanchaalɩŋ naa, nɩ ŋaaŋ vʋara nanjʋʋŋ nyaaŋ ma a taaŋ ama ta nyɩa nyɔgɩnɩ aŋaŋ nyaabʋ!

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: “Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba nɩ yiwo gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma, dama nɩ daaraa nɩ nyanyuuli kpala aŋaŋ nɩ tɩmbala kʋasɩ, ama a sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ gbatɩŋ aŋaŋ halɩ.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Nɩnɩŋ Farasisi mba yine yɩɩsɩ, nɩ woliŋ a daarɩ nɩ nyanyuuli kpalaha aŋaŋ nɩ tɩmbalaha sʋgɩtɩ, ta a kʋasɩ dɩaŋ nan daarɩ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba nɩ yiwo gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Dama nɩ sɩɩ sɩba vɔrɩtɩ dieke ba mɩɩ a penti zie, ta a kʋasɩ dɩ vɩɩna ama ta a sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ vuota kɔba aŋaŋ nyʋʋgɩŋ.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ ta vuosi yese nɩ yeŋ me ta nɩ vɩɩna, ama nɩ sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ aŋaŋ bɩaŋ. Yisa die dɩ bala ba tɩbɩdatɩŋ wɩa (Luki

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: “Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba nɩ yiwo gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Nɩ mɩa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba vɔrɩtɩ, ta penti zieŋ, ta yi jadieke dɩ baaŋ nan tɩɩnsɩ vuosi yaa gamma vuodiekemba die dɩ chɩgɩnana Ŋmɩŋ kuŋ wɩa,

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: ta baarɩ dɩ, die tɩ tɩŋ beri tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ saŋka, tɩ tɩŋ kaaŋ yi wudieke ba yine wo ta kʋʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba.

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Die wɩa nɩ seŋ a saagɩ mɩŋ dɩ nɩ yine vuodiekemba die dɩ kʋna Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba wo haagɩsɩ.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: Nɩnɩŋ nyinvuuke aŋaŋ nyinvuuke ballɩ. Nɩ baa nɩ yiwo lalɩa a chɩgɩ nyɩŋ tɩbɩdatɩŋ boli dieke dɩ wone kpisiŋ sʋŋ?

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, lele gie vuosi nan daansɩ ye tɩbɩdatɩŋ tʋmbɩatɩ gie mana wɩa. Yisa die dɩ chone Jerusalemi vuosi wɩa dene (Luki

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: N bala nɩ, a nyɩŋ jinne yaa gamma, nɩ kaaŋ bɩ ye mɩŋ bɩbra a ga tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nɩ baaŋ nan balɩ dɩ, ‘Ŋmɩŋ alibarika bie vuodieke dɩ kennene tɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ saaŋ ma.’ ”

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ jigiŋ a chʋŋ gara, die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ wa, ye Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ dɩ vɩɩna die.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Die ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩ ye nyinti gie mana mɩŋ? Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ba nan kpaŋŋɩ a mana a taaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ta taŋ balɩmɩŋ gbaŋ kaaŋ wɩarɩ a sʋgɩ ka chanchaaŋ ma.” Wumugisike aŋaŋ wahala wɩa (Maki

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: dama vuosi pam nan nagɩ n saaŋ a keŋ a baarɩ banɩŋ ba yine manɩŋ Masia wa ta gaaŋ vuosi pam.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Nɩ nan wʋŋ tɔntɩ watɩ nɩ chaaŋ aŋaŋ saasaa wɩa, ama nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, dama sie wɩaha gie yi, ama a ka dagɩ sɩba dʋnɩa kpatɩkʋ keŋye mɩŋ.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Yisa die dɩ sʋʋna a kalɩ kunkogiŋ dieke ba wasɩnana Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiŋ sikpeŋ die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ ba nyɩɩna a pɩasɩ wa, “Balɩ tɩ saŋŋa dieke genhe mana dɩ bala a yi aŋaŋ wudieke dɩ bala ka yi a dagɩ sɩba fʋ kieŋku aŋaŋ dʋnɩa kpatɩŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Vuosi buuriŋ nan wagɩ taŋ, naalɩŋ nan wagɩ naalɩŋ. Kɔŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ dɔmɩŋ nan dɩ bie jigiŋ mana.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Saŋka mi hɔgʋ nyuutitieliŋ aŋaŋ vuodieke dɩ yalla ballɩnyaalɩsɩ nan dii wahala pam!

ref: Jesus answered: Don't let anyone fool you.
text: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Nɩ sɩmma aŋ vuoŋ da keŋ gaaŋ nɩ,

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Wɩaha gie mana nan dɩ sɩɩ sɩba maarɩŋ yʋagɩŋ piiliŋ.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: “Saŋka mi ba nan yigi nɩ a nagɩ nɩ yɩ aŋ ba vaa nɩ dii wahala, ta kʋʋ nɩ. Tɩŋgbaŋɩsɩ mana nan dɩ haa nɩ dama nɩ dɩna n kʋaŋ wɩa.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Saŋka mi vuosi pam nan zeti n kʋaŋ dɩɩsɩŋ, ba nan posi tamba chɩasɩ ta haa tamba.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ta ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ naazʋalɩba nan keŋ a gaaŋ vuosi pam.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Saŋka mi dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a balɩ nɩ dɩ, ‘Nɩ ye, Masia wa wʋnna yaa ʋ benne mi wo’, nɩ daa keŋ yi wo yada;

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ aŋ ʋ da keŋ yi wahalaha keŋ yɩbɩŋ saŋŋa yaa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: “Nɩ nan ye ‘Jachʋʋsɩ’ dieke ta Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Danieli die dɩ balla ka wɩa wa dɩ, genke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ chʋarɩŋ, ka nan dɩ zie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ.”

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: “Saŋka mi vuodiekemba dɩ benne Judia ma ma wa, chɩgɩ a ga jʋalɩ tana sikpeŋ.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ keŋ bie ʋ kʋaŋ ma, ʋ da keŋ kuli dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ jayeekiŋ.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Ŋmɩŋ dɩ tɩŋ ka gobi daraaha a taaŋ vuoŋ tɩŋ kaaŋ ye gbatɩtaanɩŋ; ama ʋ vuovʋarɩkaha wɩa ta ʋ nan gobi daraaha a taaŋ.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Nɩ bɩ a keŋ ye ta nyinti gie yie naa gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ n keniŋ gbigiye mɩŋ.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Die dɩ sɩna die wo Nowa saŋka dɩ nan dɩ sɩɩ die manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa keniŋ saŋŋa.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “Ama vuoŋ ka sɩba daarɩ yaa saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ keŋ; malakasi dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩaŋ ka sɩba, manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa gbaŋ ka sɩba, sie n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna sɩbɩna.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Nowa saŋŋa, saŋŋa dieke nyaakpɩɩbʋ dɩ yene ka keŋye, vuosi die dɩ die ta nyuo, ta faara hɔɔŋ, ta naga ba hɔgɩlɩasɩ a yɩa chʋllɩŋ, a ga tʋgɩ daa dieke Nowa dɩ june haarɩkpɩɩkʋ sʋŋ.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Ta vuosi gie die ka sɩba wudieke dɩ yinene a tʋgɩ saŋŋa dieke nyaabʋ dɩ kenne dii ba mana. Die dɩ baa dɩ sɩmma saŋŋa dieke manɩŋ vuota Bʋa wa dɩ bala n keŋ wo.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Saŋka mi dembisi bale nan dɩ tʋma kʋakʋ ma, ta n nan nagɩ wʋnyɩ ta va wʋnyɩ.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Die wɩa nɩ gbara dama nɩ zɩ daa dieke manɩŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala n keŋ.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Ama ʋ yi yɔmʋbɩaŋ, ʋ nan dɩ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo kaaŋ keŋ lagɩ lagɩ,

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: “Yɔmʋ bɩa yine yɩantieŋ ta yie ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ dagɩna dɩ ʋ yi wudieke? Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuodieke ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ nan vʋarɩ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba ta yɩma ba ba nyindiike saŋŋa dieke dɩ mʋna.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Dɩɩ yi ta ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ keŋ tigiŋ ta ye wo ta ʋ tʋma die wo gbaŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ nan dɩ bie ʋ ma.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ʋ nan nagɩ wa a zieŋ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ nyinti mana mana.

ref: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
text: ta piili a nɩga ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba ta wʋnɩŋ ʋ nan dɩ die ta nyuo aŋaŋ daanyuuriŋ.

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: Ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo nan yiŋŋi keŋ daa dieke aŋaŋ saŋŋa dieke ʋ kana ka yile dɩ ʋ nan keŋ.

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Ʋ keŋ keŋ, ʋ nan datɩ ʋ tɩbɩŋ ta nagɩ wa a gʋtɩ gɩgaantɩba ma; mi ʋ nan dɩ kʋma ta ŋɔba nyɩna.

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Gaantɩba die dɩ nagɩ ba popolisi ama ta die ka nagɩ kpaaŋ a gʋtɩ pɔgɩlɩ,

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: ama ta sɩsɩbɩrɩba die dɩ nagɩ kpaaŋ a siti siti a yi kɔlɩbɩsɩ ma a gʋtɩ pɔgɩlɩ aŋaŋ popolisi.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: “Ta die tanseese die ba wʋŋ kuli; ‘Hɔgʋ faarɩtʋ wa wʋnna! Nɩ keŋ nyɩŋ a tuoli wo!’

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: Saŋka mi hɔgʋlɩaba baŋka mana die dɩ hagɩ a ŋmɩɩlɩ ba popoli dɩ jʋalɩ.

ref: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
text: Die hɔgʋlɩa gaantɩba die dɩ jʋʋsɩ sɩsɩbɩrɩba dɩ, ‘Nɩ kaaŋ yɩ tɩ nɩ kpaabʋ bɩta? Dama tɩ popolisi yaala a kpi mɩŋ.’

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: Ta sɩsɩbɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, ‘Aayɩ, bʋ kaaŋ tʋgɩ tɩnɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ mana. Nɩ gamma vuodiekemba dɩ yalla daa wa jigiŋ a daa bʋtaŋ yɩ nɩ gbaŋ.’

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Die Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, “Die wɩa, nɩ sɩmma, dama nɩ ka sɩba daa dieke yaa saŋŋa dieke manɩŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala n keŋ. Yɔŋɩsɩ bataa wɩa (Luki

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: “Die dɩ wana a yʋasɩ pam, tigiri tieŋ wo die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ, ta wa ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ dɩ ba keŋ aŋ ʋ biisi, ta ʋ ye ba yene nyʋarɩ a mʋ die.
