text: Die hɔgʋba die yene be sieŋ me a gara, ta sojasisi bataŋ die dɩ gbarɩna vɔrɩkʋ dɩ yiŋŋi a ga tɩka ma a balɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha wudieke dɩ yine.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: a balɩ ba, “Nɩ balɩ dɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba kenne tanseese saŋŋa dieke tɩ dʋana gʋʋra a keŋ gaarɩ ʋ nyɩŋgbaŋka.

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Die gbɩgbarɩtɩba dɩ tuo ligirehe ta die ga balɩ vuosi wudieke ba balla dɩ ba balɩ wa. Die wɩa ta Juu vuosi die dɩ tuo wudieke ba bala wa a dii a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne. Yisa die dɩ nyɩnna ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ dene

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Die ʋ keŋ gbigi be a baarɩ dɩ, “Ŋmɩŋ yɩ mɩŋ wa ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ aŋaŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie yiko mana mana mɩŋ.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Die wɩa nɩ gamma tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi buuriŋ mana jigiŋ a yi be n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩŋ, ta sɩma ba Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ saaŋ ma, aŋaŋ manɩŋ ʋ Bʋadembiŋ saaŋ ma, aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ Halɩkasɩka saaŋ ma,

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: Die ba yene wo die ba gbirigi a jɩaŋ wa ama bataŋ die dɩ chɩɩlɩ wa.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Lagɩ lagɩ ba vaa ba nɩɩtɩ ta hagɩ a dɩ wa.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Ta bɩ ga nɩŋŋa bɩta ta a ye Jemisi aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn, Zebedi ballɩ, ta ba bie wo ba haarɩŋ sʋŋ a wʋnsa ba nɩɩtɩ.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa, Yisa saaŋ die dɩ dɩa Galili tɩŋgbaŋ mana. Yisa dɩ gbaanna daadaŋ pam Dene (Mat.

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: Sɩba Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ maagɩna die wo: “Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ, ‘Nɩ ye, n baaŋ n tʋŋ wa n tʋntʋntʋ aŋ ʋ woliŋ a dɩa fʋ nɩŋŋa a wʋnsɩ fʋ sieti a yɩ fʋ.’

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Jɔɔn die yeegi wo nyiŋyeeki diekemba die ba nagɩna nyɔgɩnɩ kobiti a wʋnsɩ, ta bɩ bɔbɩ gbanɩŋ ʋ chɩaŋ ma, die ʋ nyindiike die dɩ yi sankpara aŋaŋ siesi.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ vuosisi dɩ, “Vuodieke dɩ hagɩrɩna a tɩaŋ mɩŋ balla ʋ keŋ n kʋaŋ chaaŋ, n ka tʋgɩ vuodieke dɩ baaŋ nan gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa ta vʋarɩ ʋ nɩɩra guune gbaŋ.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga tɩŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Kapenɔmi; ta Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ dɩ keŋ ba ga Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juoku me, ʋ piili a daga vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Ʋ dagɩkʋ gie die dɩ yi be mamachi pam, dama ʋ dagɩ ba sɩba vuodieke dɩ yalla yiko, die ka ka sɩɩ sɩba Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ ŋaana a daga ba wa.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Die dɩ ka yʋasɩya die Yisa dɩ nyɩŋ Nazeriti tɩŋ dɩ benne Galili me wo a keŋ Jɔɔn jigiŋ, ta Jɔɔn die dɩ sɩɩ wa Ŋmɩŋ nyaaŋ Jɔɔdani mʋgɩrɩ ma.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Yisa die dɩ nyɩnnana nyaabʋ ma wa ʋ ye ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ dɩ yuorine, Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ dɩ keŋ a sʋʋna ʋ sikpeŋ me sɩba ŋmarɩŋ.

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Dembiŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ gobine gamɩŋ die dɩ keŋ a gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa, ta jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ suŋŋi wo. Gaŋkʋ nɩŋ die dɩ baarɩ, “Dɩɩ yi fʋ dʋŋŋʋ fʋ gbaaŋ mɩŋ.”

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: a baarɩ “Saŋŋa dieke Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ dɩ balla ka keŋ wo gbigiye mɩŋ, die wɩa nɩ yi Ŋmɩŋ wʋvɩɩnaha gie yada ta va nɩ wʋbɩatɩ.”

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Ʋ wa ba, die ba va ba chɔɔŋ wa Zebedi aŋaŋ vuosisi die dɩ tʋnnana a yɩa ba wa ta hagɩ a dɩ wa. Dembi dieke jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ yalla wa wɩa (Luki

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Yisa dɩ ga a yigi ʋ nuuŋ ta suŋŋi wo ʋ hagɩ kalɩ, ʋ yʋagɩbʋ mana dɩ kpatɩ ta ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ fɩalɩ, ta die ʋ hagɩ a yi chaantɩ a yɩ ba.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: die ba yene wo wo ba balɩ wa, “Vuoŋ mana yaala ba ye jigidieke fʋ benne.”

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Womi ta dembi dieke jɩmbɩakʋ dɩ yalla wa die dɩ keŋ a juu Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juoku sʋŋ,

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: a keesi pam ta baarɩ dɩ, “Yisa, vuodieke dɩ nyɩnna Nazeriti, bɩa fʋ yaala tɩ jigiŋ? Keŋ dɩ fʋ chʋʋsɩ tɩ mɩŋ? N sɩba vuodieke dɩ yine fʋ, yiwo Ŋmɩŋ tʋntʋŋ kasɩ vuoŋ.”

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Ama ta Yisa die dɩ natɩ wa, “Tarɩ watɩ ta va wa!”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ paalɩ a dɔŋ ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ pam ta taaŋ keesiŋ pam ta die nyɩŋ ʋ ma.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Die, die dɩ yi ba mana mamachi ta ba pɩasa ba chanchaalɩŋ dɩ, “Bɩa wʋnna? A yiwo wʋhaala? Ʋ yaa yiko ta yɩa jɩmbɩatɩ nʋaŋ ta a tuose nʋarɩ!”

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Ama ta Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Sie tɩ ga tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ diekemba dɩ gbigine giena wa. Ntaala n balɩ Ŋmɩŋ wɩa mi gbaŋ, dama die wɩa maŋ keŋ.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Zɔɔlɩŋ die dɩ yigi Yisa ʋ tɩɩntɩ ʋ nuuŋ a gbɩ wa ta balɩ wa, “N saagɩya. Fʋ yʋagɩbʋ kpatɩya mɩŋ.”

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Die ba dene a nyɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juoku sʋŋ die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ Jemisi aŋaŋ Jɔɔn die dɩ ga a juu Simoni aŋaŋ Andurusi tigiŋ me.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Ta Simoni hanɩɩŋ hɔgʋ die dɩ dʋa a yʋagɩ, die ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ dɩ faasɩ tuuli, ba balɩ Yisa ʋ yʋagɩbʋ gie wɩa.

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Ta dɩnda daarɩ mi ŋmɩŋ die dɩ june, vuotamba die dɩ chii yʋagɩtieliŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba jɩmbɩatɩ dɩ yalla ba a kieŋ Yisa jigiŋ.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Ta tɩka gie vuosi mana die dɩ lagɩsɩ a keŋ suuli tigiri me.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Die ʋ vaa vuodiekemba die dɩ yʋagɩna yʋagɩtɩ yiri yiri mana dɩ ye gbaamɩŋ, ta bɩ yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩ vuotamba pam ma, ama ʋ ka saaga dɩ jɩmbɩatɩ balɩ wɩɩŋ, dama die a sɩba vuodieke ʋ yine mɩŋ. Yisa die dɩ balla wʋvɩɩna Galili tɩka ma dene (Luki

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Yisa die dɩ woliŋ a nyɩŋ tigiri gie me ta kparaasɩ ye ka kʋma, ta ga haagɩŋ ma a ga jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ.

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Ta Simoni aŋaŋ ʋ chanchaalɩba die dɩ dɩa a yaala ʋ jigiŋ,

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die ʋ dɩdɩa Galili tɩka mana mana, a ga bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ jʋʋsɩŋ juone nɩŋ mana ta die a yaga jɩmbɩatɩ vuosi me. Yisa die dɩ gbaanna gamɩŋ dene (Mat.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ yi ʋ ga lagɩ lagɩ ama ta a paalɩ a balɩ wa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ,

ref: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
text: “Wʋmma, sɩmma fʋ keŋ a balɩ vuoŋ wɩaha gie. Ama gamma a ga nagɩ fʋ gbaŋ a dagɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtʋ wa a vaa ʋ jɩɩŋ fʋ aŋ fʋ kaabɩ kaabɩ dieke Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ balla Mosisi yaa gamma gbaanɩŋ jigiri, aŋ vuoŋ mana seŋ a sɩmma a baarɩ wusie fʋ yʋagɩtɩtɩ kpatɩya mɩŋ.”

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Ama daa wa die dɩ ga a bala wɩaha gie jigiŋ mana mana. Wusie, die ʋ seŋ ga a balɩ wa die amʋ Yisa die ka bɩ a bɩagɩ juu tɩka sʋŋ vuosi me. Ta wɩarɩ jigidieke vuosi die dɩ worine, vuosi die dɩ nyɩna jigiŋ mana mana a kieŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: die wɩa die ʋ ga a juu Ŋmɩŋ tigiri me a ŋɔbɩ paanʋ dieke die ba nagɩna a yɩ Ŋmɩnnɩ. Saŋka mi Abiata die yine Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ. Ama tɩ mɩra die ka baarɩ vuogaasɩ ŋɔbɩha sie Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩɩna ma; ama ta Davidi die dɩ ŋɔbɩ paanʋkʋ gie, halɩ ta die a nagɩ ataŋ a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gbaŋ ba ŋɔbɩ.”

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Die vuosi die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ. Die wɩa jigiŋ die bɩ wori aŋaŋ vuogaasɩ dɩ baaŋ nan dɩ beri, die juoku mana suuliye mɩŋ aŋaŋ yeŋ me mana. Die ʋ bala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa a yɩa ba.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Womi ta dembisi banɩɩsa wonde a chii vuodieke nyɩŋgbanɩŋ mana dɩ kpine a kieŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: Die daadamba dɩ dala wa chɩaŋ ma die ba ka bɩagɩ a yaa wa tʋgɩ ʋ jigiŋ. Ba yuori toli mampili dieke ma ma Yisa Masia dɩ benne wo. Ba wana yuori toli mampili me a kpatɩ wa, ba nagɩ daa wa aŋaŋ giliŋgiliŋke a yaa sʋʋŋ dʋaŋ Yisa nɩŋŋa.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: dɩ “Wʋbɩa daa wa gie dɩ bala die? Ʋ bala sɩba ʋ yi wo Ŋmɩŋ. Vuobɩa baaŋ nan nagɩ vuoŋ taalɩ a chaa wa ntaala Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna?”

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: N nan wa a dagɩ aŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa yaa yiko dʋnɩa ma ma a nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ vuosi tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba.” Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ vuodieke nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ kpine wo,

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “N bala fʋ, hagɩ a nagɩ fʋ giliŋgiliŋke, a kuli.”

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa dɩ bɩ yiŋŋi ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gie lʋgɩŋ ta vuosi pam dɩ lagɩsɩ ʋ jigiŋ a giliŋ wo, ta ʋ daga ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Womi die ʋ balɩ a kpatɩ ba dɩ, “Vuotaŋ wɩa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ naaŋ davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ ama daa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daarɩ wɩa ʋ naaŋ vuotaŋ.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Die kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ die nyindiike Liiva tigiŋ me, lampotuosiriŋ pam aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Juu vuosisi mɩraha pam die dɩdɩ Yisa kʋaŋ. Ba pam die dɩ keŋ a gʋtɩ ba ma aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyindiikehe jigiŋ.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ die dɩ yine Farasisi die dɩ yene Yisa aŋaŋ lampotuosiribe aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Juu vuosisi mɩraha die dɩ lagɩsɩna tamba a die nyindiikehe, die ba pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, “Bɩa yine wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ kana ka dɩ Juu vuosisi mɩraha mi chanchaalɩŋ dɩ lagɩsa a die?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Saŋŋa kaanɩ, Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ vuosi bataŋ ba wasɩnana Farasisi die dɩ bɔba nʋaŋ. Vuosi bataŋ die dɩ keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Bɩa yine Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi dɩ bɔba nʋaŋ, ta fʋnɩŋ nɩŋ fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ka bɔba?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba, “Nɩ tɩŋ yaala chaaŋ diekemba dɩ gana hɔgʋ faarɩŋ ma bɔbɩ nʋaŋ? Banɩŋ aŋaŋ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ dɩ lagɩsɩna a beri wo, die kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yi.

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Ama saŋŋa nan keŋ ba nan nagɩ hɔgʋ wa tieŋ a nyɩŋ ba jigiŋ, saŋka mi ba nan wa bɔbɩ nʋaŋ.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Vuoŋ kaaŋ saagɩ a nagɩ garɩhaalɩŋ garɩchɔɔtɩŋ a baalɩ a ligi garɩkʋʋŋ vɔrɩŋ, dama garɩhaalɩkʋ gie nan chɩɩrɩ ta vaa garɩkʋʋrɩ, aŋ ka vɔrɩkʋ wa a faasɩ a dalɩma.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Yaa nɩ yeye ta vuoŋ nagɩ daahaalɩŋ a sʋʋ gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋkʋra ma? Dama ʋ yi die daabʋ nan kʋŋ a chɩɩrɩ kolititi aŋ daabʋ aŋaŋ kolititi mana taaŋ yɔrɩ. Ama ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa daahaalɩŋ a sʋʋ gbanɩŋ koliti diekemba dɩ yine nyɩŋhaala ma.” Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ wɩa (Mat.

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Tɔ, Juu vuosi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ, Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ chʋŋ tɩanna zaa kʋaŋ ma. Die ba ganana wa die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ gobe zaapakɩtɩ a yɩaga ŋɔba ta gara.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Die wɩa Farasisi vuosi die dɩ pɩasɩ dɩ, “Ye, bɩa wɩa ba yie wudieke tɩ mɩraha dɩ kana ka saaga dɩ ba yi davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ ba, “Nɩ ye ka karɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ gbaŋkʋ sʋŋ Davidi aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yine wudieke kɔŋ die dɩ yalla ba wa? Kɔŋ die yigine wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba,

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Die wɩa, manɩŋ vuota Bʋadembiŋ yine davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ jakʋʋŋ.”

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Ta ʋ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba nagɩ haarɩŋ a yɩ wa aŋ ʋ juu a kala ta dagɩ vuosi gie Ŋmɩŋ wɩa, amʋ ba kaaŋ bɩagɩ a kpaŋŋɩ wa,

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Farasisi vuosi dɩaŋ die benne mi a yaala wɩɩŋ ba nagɩ a yigi Yisa, die wɩa die ba paalɩ a paala wa dɩ ba ye sɩba ʋ nan gbaaŋ daa wa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yisa dɩ balɩ a yɩ nuukpiikiri tieŋ, “Keŋ tɩaŋ a zie nɩŋŋa.”

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: Yisa die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ ta daansɩ ba a keŋ tɩaŋ aŋaŋ die mana yɔrɩ ta die ʋ chɩgɩ ba zɔɔlɩŋ dama die ba yiwo vuotɔgɩtɩ. Womi ʋ balɩ daa wa, “Tɩɩrɩ fʋ nuuke.”

ref: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
text: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ hagɩ a sʋʋŋ ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋgaha ta vuotamba pam die dɩ dɩɩ ʋ kʋaŋ, vuodiekemba die dɩ nyɩnna Galili aŋaŋ Judia,

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ jʋalɩ taŋ sikpeŋ ta wa wa vuodiekemba ʋ yaalɩnana dɩ ba keŋ, die ba keŋ ʋ jigiŋ.

ref: and to force out demons.
text: ta bɩ yallɩma yiko a vʋarɩma jɩmbɩatɩ vuotamba ma.

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: Jemisi aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn, vuodiekemba die dɩ yine Zebedi ballɩ; ta Yisa die dɩ yɩ ba Bʋanejesi, ka chɩaŋ yiwo nɩɩŋ natɩŋ ballɩ;

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: aŋaŋ Andurusi aŋaŋ Filipi, aŋaŋ Batolomiwo, aŋaŋ Matiwo, aŋaŋ Tomasi, aŋaŋ Jemisi vuodieke die dɩ yine Alifusi bʋa aŋaŋ Tadeusi, aŋaŋ Simoni vuodieke die dɩ yaalala banɩŋ Juu vuosi yallɩma ba gbaŋ

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Die Ŋmɩŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ nyɩnna Jerusalemi die dɩ baarɩ dɩ, “Jɩmbɩatɩ naaŋ wa Beelizebuli die yalla wa ʋ bɩagɩ vʋara jɩmbɩatɩ daadamba ma, dɩ yiwo Beelizebuli yiko wɩa.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Daadamba die dɩ taanna a giliŋ wo wo die dɩ balɩ a yɩ wa, “Fʋ naa aŋaŋ fʋ nɩmballɩ bie yeŋ me a yaala fʋ.”

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Die Yisa die dɩ wa dɩdagɩrɩba gie dɩ ba keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, ta die yɩ ba nandagɩŋ, a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Sitaani nan saagɩ a vʋara ʋ gbaŋ vuosi me?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Naa kʋaŋ chaaŋ Yisa die dɩ kuli; ta daadamba pam die dɩ lagɩsɩ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba jigiŋ ta die ka yaa saŋŋa dɩ ba dii nyindiike.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Naaŋ vuosi dɩ yiŋŋi a waga tamba, ba naarɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zie.

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Ta dɩɩ yi tigiŋ vuosi dɩ yiŋŋi a waga tamba, ba tigiri kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zie.

ref: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
text: Ta Sitaani dɩ yiŋŋi a waga aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zie, ʋ kpatɩŋ wonde. Yisa die dɩ chʋʋsɩna Sitaani hagɩrɩŋ wɩa dene

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: “Vuoŋ wori a baaŋ bɩagɩ a ga hagɩrɩtieŋ tigiŋ me a ga gaarɩ ʋ nyinti, sie ʋ woliŋ bɔbɩ wa ta ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ bɩ gaarɩ ʋ tigiŋ nyinti mana.”

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Yisa dɩ bɩ balɩ ba dɩ, “Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, Ŋmɩŋ nan vaa chaa vuosi ba wʋbɩatɩ aŋaŋ ba wʋtɔgɩbɩatɩ mana;

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: ama vuodieke mana dɩ balɩ wʋtɔgɩbɩatɩ a yɩ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩŋ, Ŋmɩŋ kaaŋ bɩ nagɩ ʋ taalɩ a chaa wa naada yaa gamma haahuu.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Yisa die balɩ wa wɩaha gie, dama die ba baarɩ dɩ, “Jɩmbɩaŋ die yalla wa.” Yisa nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ wɩa (Mat.

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Saŋka mi Yisa nuŋ wo aŋaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ die dɩ keŋ ta zie yeŋ me, ta tʋŋ vuoŋ dɩ ʋ wa wa mi.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Ʋ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Mɩnɩa yine n naa, ta mɩnɩa yine n nɩmballɩ?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Ta daansɩ daadamba die dɩ kalla ʋ jigiri ta baarɩ dɩ, “N nuŋ aŋaŋ n nɩmballɩ benne giena.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Vuodieke mana dɩ yinene Ŋmɩŋ choti, wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine n nɩmbʋa aŋaŋ n taa aŋaŋ n nuŋ.”

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Saŋŋa kaanɩ bɩbra die Yisa die dɩ kalɩ a daga wɩa Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ lʋgɩŋ chaaŋ. Daadamba die dɩ gilinne wo die paalɩ a dala mɩŋ die wɩa die ʋ hagɩ a ga juu haarɩkʋ sʋŋ a bie nyaaŋ sʋŋ, ta vaa vuosisi a zieŋ nyaabʋ kʋanʋaŋ.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Die ʋ bʋrɩnana ʋ kʋakʋ ma wa, nyɩŋbʋraha ataŋ die dɩ nan sieku me, nembisi die dɩ keŋ diihe.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Die ʋ nagɩ nandaga a dagɩ ba wɩa pam.

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ʋ dagɩkʋ sʋŋ die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ: “Nɩ wʋmma, daa wʋnyɩ die nyɩnna a ga dɩ ʋ bʋrɩ ʋ kʋaŋ ma.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Nyɩŋbʋra ataŋ die dɩ nan tanjaalɩŋ ma jigidieke tantɩ pam die dɩ worine. Nyɩŋbʋraha die dɩ nyʋʋŋ lagɩ lagɩ, dama tantɩ die ka dala.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ zie die a mana die dɩ kpi dama die a dagɩrɩsɩ ka sʋʋŋya.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Ataŋ die dɩ nan haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ ma, haŋgɔɔsɩ die dɩ bɩrɩŋ a nyagɩha a ka bɩagɩ yi nyɩŋbʋra.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Bɩa jaaŋ tɩ baa tɩ nagɩ Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ a magɩsɩ? Yaa nandagɩ bɩa tɩ baa tɩ nagɩ a dagɩ ka chɩaŋ aŋ vuoŋ mana sɩmma?

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: ama a ka dʋaga ba sʋgɩtɩ ma a yʋasa, dama wɩɩrɩ gie ka sʋʋŋ ba sʋgɩtɩ ma ma, die wɩa ba keŋ mugisi be yaa wahala dɩ keŋ juu be wɩaha gie chɩaŋ ma ba ŋaaŋ nagɩha a taaŋ mɩŋ lagɩ lagɩ.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Amʋ ‘Ba nan dɩ daansa a daansa ama ba kaaŋ ye jaaŋ; ba nan wʋŋ a bɩ wʋŋ a gʋtɩ ama ba kaaŋ dɩ sɩba ka chɩaŋ. Dama ba keŋ a sɩba a chɩasɩ ba nan yiŋŋi a keŋ Ŋmɩŋ jigiŋ, aŋ Ŋmɩŋ tɩŋ nagɩ ba tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba.’ ” Nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Nyindiekemba die dɩ nanna sieku me wo yi wo vuodiekemba dɩ ŋaana a wʋŋ Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha ama dɩ ka yʋasa ta Sitaani keŋ vʋarɩha ba ma.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Bʋbʋrɩtʋ wa nɩasɩ wa vuodieke dɩ balala Ŋmɩŋ wɩa.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Vuoŋ nan chogisi popoli a zieŋ juoŋ sʋŋ ta ligike aŋaŋ kpalɩ, yaa ta nagɩka a zieŋ gado chɩaŋ? Aayɩ, ʋ nan nagɩka a zieŋ jaaŋ sikpeŋ.

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a baarɩ dɩ, “Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ nɩasɩ wa sɩba vuoŋ dɩ bʋrɩna ʋ nyɩŋbʋra ʋ kʋaŋ ma,

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Nyɩŋbʋraha dɩ keŋ a bii kʋakʋ tieŋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ wa ʋ jɩbɩŋ a piili a gobe dama a gobiŋ saŋŋa tʋgɩya mɩŋ.” Nyɩŋbʋra nandagɩrɩ

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Ka sɩɩ sɩba kaŋmɩnɩŋ biŋ vuoŋ dɩ nagɩna a bʋrɩ tɩŋgbaŋ ma, ka yi jabɩŋ a tɩaŋ nyɩŋbʋra mana mana dʋnɩa ma ma,

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: ama dɩ ka yʋasa ta ka nyuuŋ a bɩrɩŋ a tɩaŋ dʋnɩa vapɔgɩtɩ mana. Ka ŋaaŋ faasɩ yaa wa dachagɩsɩkpɩɩma pam aŋaŋ nembisi dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a yʋgɩ ba tuosi ka sikpeŋ.”

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Die ʋ ka balɩ wɩɩŋ a yɩa ba ta ka nagɩ nandagɩŋ a balɩ, ama saŋŋa dieke nɩŋ mana die wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba nyɩɩna ma dɩ keŋ a kalɩ, ʋ ŋaaŋ dagɩ ba mɩŋ nandagaha mana chɩasɩ. Bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ die dɩ tuone Yisa nʋarɩ dene

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Yisa die nagɩ wa nandaga nɩasa sɩba naa, a dagɩ ba Ŋmɩŋ wɩa pam. Die ʋ dagɩ ba wa nandaga diekemba ba baaŋ nan dɩ sɩba a chɩasɩ.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Die wɩa die ba va daadamba mi ta die a juu haarɩkʋ Yisa die wone bie ka sʋŋ, banɩŋ aŋaŋ Yisa dɩ ga. Haarɩsɩ ataŋ gbaŋ die benne mi.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Womi bʋlɔgɩsɩkpeŋkpɩɩŋ die dɩ piili a nɩga; nyaabʋ die dɩ poŋŋi a juo haarɩkʋ sʋŋ. Die wɩa die haarɩkʋ die dɩ yaala ka suuli aŋaŋ nyaaŋ.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Yisa die bie wo haarɩkʋ kʋaŋ a nagɩ ʋ sikpeŋ a kpagɩlɩ dafere a gʋʋra. Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ sʋgɩrɩ wa a baarɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ, dɩ ka mugisi fʋ tɩ balla tɩ kpi naa?”

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Yisa die dɩ hagɩ a yɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ nʋaŋ, “Vaa nɩgɩŋ” ta balɩ a yɩ nyaaŋ poŋŋiku dɩaŋ “Zie dene.” Bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ nan, mi mana die dɩ paalɩ fɩalɩ.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Womi die Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ, “Bɩa yine ŋmaamɩŋ dɩ yallɩ nɩ, nɩ ye ko wo yada?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Ama ta ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ a yallɩ ba, die ba pɩasa tamba, “Mɩnɩa yine daa wa gie? Halɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ poŋŋiŋ dɩ wʋma ʋ wʋbalɩka yɩa wa.”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ʋ gbaŋ dɩ, “Maŋ tɩŋ a ko a bɩagɩ a gbɩ ʋ nyiŋyeekehe gie n tɩŋ nan ye alaafɩa.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Yisa die dɩ balɩ wa, “N lɩa, fʋ yadaka gbaaŋ fʋ. Gamma aŋaŋ sʋgɩdʋagɩŋ, fʋ yʋagɩbʋ kaaŋ bɩ a yiŋŋi a keŋ.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Die ʋ paalɩ a ga wa gbɩgbaantɩŋ pam jige dɩ ʋ ye gbaamɩŋ. Die ʋ dii ʋ ligire mana mana a ga kpatɩ, ama ʋ tɩŋ baaŋ nan ye alaafɩaka die dɩ wa a paalɩ a mugise wo daaŋ mana mana.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Die ʋ ga a juu a pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Dɩ bɩa watɩ gie mana? Dɩ bɩa nɩ kʋma? Bʋa wa ka kpiye, ʋ ta ko gʋʋra mɩŋ.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ jʋalɩ a keŋ tʋgɩ Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ chaakʋ mi, Gerasa tɩŋgbaŋ ma.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Jɩmbɩaŋ die yalla wa, daa wa gie die dɩ ga gʋʋra kunti vɔrɩtɩtɩ ma, die ba ka bɩagɩ a bɔbɩ wa a dʋaŋ aŋaŋ chɔrɩma.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Dama ba keŋ bɔbɩ wa ʋ nuusi aŋaŋ ʋ nagɩsɩ ʋ ŋaaŋ kpaa kpaa chɔrɩmaha mɩŋ ʋ nuusi me ta wɩɩ kʋʋtɩtɩ ʋ nagɩsɩ ma. Die ʋ paalɩ a hagɩrɩ mɩŋ aŋaŋ vuoŋ dɩ baaŋ nan nyaŋŋɩ wa.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Daaŋ mana sʋkʋʋŋ aŋaŋ yuŋ ʋ ŋaaŋ dɩ chʋŋ wa vɔrɩtɩ aŋaŋ kunkogehe me a nata ta naga tana a gobe ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Die ʋ bie wo saasaa ta die a ye Yisa, die wɩa die ʋ chɩgɩ a ga gbirigi ʋ dune me Yisa nɩŋŋa,

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: ta faasɩ a taaŋ keesiŋ a baarɩ dɩ, “Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ Bʋadembiŋ Yisa, bɩa fʋ yaala n jigiŋ, Ŋmɩŋ wɩa daa keŋ datɩ n tɩbɩŋ.”

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Perukusi pam die gbigine mi a die nyinti kunkogiri mi chaaŋ.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Yisa dɩ, “Vaa tɩ gamma perukusisi jigiŋ a ga juu ba sʋgɩtɩ.”

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Dembiŋ diekemba die dɩ jɩɩnana perukusisi die chɩgɩ a ga tɩka sʋŋ aŋaŋ ka tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ a balɩ wɩɩrɩ gie a yɩ ba. Vuosi die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga a ye wudieke die dɩ yine wo.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Die ba ga Yisa jigiŋ a ye daa dieke die jɩmbɩakʋ die dɩ yalla wa ta ʋ kallɩ wa mi a yeegi nyiŋyeeke ta ʋ sikpeŋ dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tʋma, ŋmaamɩŋ die dɩ yallɩ ba mana mana.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Die wɩa die vuosi dɩ piili a jʋʋsa Yisa dɩ ʋ nyɩŋ ba tɩka sʋŋ.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Ama Yisa die dɩ ka saagɩya, ta yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, “Yiŋŋi a ga fʋ dembe jigiŋ a balɩ ba Nabidie Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala fʋ wɩa dene ta piili a yi wudieke a yɩ fʋ.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Die wɩa daa wa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ baŋ wa mana mana a balɩ wudieke Yisa die dɩ yine a yɩ wa wa. Vuodiekemba mana mana die dɩ wʋnna ʋ balɩkʋ die dɩ yi be mamachi. Havʋʋbiŋ dieke dɩ kpine aŋaŋ hɔgʋ yʋagɩtieŋ wɩa

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Die ba piili a vʋara wa fala. Die wɩa die ʋ yagɩ ba mana a nyɩŋ ta die a wa bʋa wa chɔɔŋ aŋaŋ ʋ nuŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba ba bataa wa a ga juu jigidieke bʋa wa die dɩ dʋana wa.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Yisa die dɩ bɩ yiŋŋi a jʋalɩ a ga mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ mi wo chaaŋ, daadaŋ pam die dɩ keŋ mili wo.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Daa wʋnyɩ ba wasɩnana Jaarusi, ta die yi Juu vuosi Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku nyɩŋkʋraha wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ mi. Die ʋ dene ye Yisa wa, ʋ nan a gbirigi ʋ dune me,

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: ta jʋʋsa wa aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna ta baarɩ dɩ, “N hɔgʋlɩabiŋ faasɩna a yʋagɩ. N jʋʋsa fʋ, keŋ a nagɩ fʋ nuusi a diisi ʋ ma aŋ ʋ ye alaafɩa a bemme ʋ mɩsɩ ma.”

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die benne mi zɩŋ dɩ nyɩna ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ ma, die dɩ faasɩ a wʋŋ wa mɩŋ bɩna baŋ aŋaŋ ale.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Die ʋ wʋnna Yisa wɩa wa die ʋ ga ʋ jigiŋ a dɩɩ ʋ kʋaŋ daadamba sʋŋ.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Die ʋ gbɩ Yisa nyiŋyeekehe gie die ʋ nyɩŋbaŋka zɩmbʋ dɩ gobi bʋnyɩ, die ʋ mɩŋŋɩ ʋ sʋŋ ma sɩba ʋ wahala mana nyɩŋya mɩŋ.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Die dɩ dene a yi, Yisa die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ lagɩ lagɩ sɩba hagɩrɩŋ nyɩŋ ʋ ma ma mɩŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ yiŋŋi a pɩasɩ daadamba dɩ, “Mɩnɩa gbɩna n nyiŋyeekehe?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋi balɩ dɩ, “Ye daadamba dɩ milinene fʋ die, dɩ bɩa fʋ pɩasa dɩ mɩnɩa gbɩna fʋ?”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Ama ta Yisa dɩ ye ko a daansa a tɩanna dɩ ʋ ye vuodieke dɩ yine die wo.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ sɩbɩna wudieke die dɩ yine wo die ʋ keŋ a cheele aŋaŋ ŋmaamɩŋ ta gbirigi ʋ dune me a balɩ wusie a yɩ wa.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Yisa die dɩ yene ko bala naa wa, tʋntʋntɩŋ bataŋ die dɩ nyɩŋ Jaarusi tigiŋ a keŋ balɩ wa dɩ, “Fʋ lɩa wa yʋagɩbʋ chʋʋsɩya mɩŋ, dɩ bɩa fʋ bɩ a daama dɩdagɩrʋ wa bɩbra?”

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yisa baga die wo ba balɩkʋ sʋŋ ta die a balɩ a yɩ Jaarusi dɩ, “Da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla fʋ, fʋnɩŋ ko tuo dii.”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Womi die ʋ yi Piita aŋaŋ Jemisi, aŋaŋ Jemisi nɩmbʋa Jɔɔn nyɩɩna ma die dɩ dɩɩ wa.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Die ba gana a tʋgɩ Jaarusi tigiri, die Yisa die dɩ ye jigiŋ mana die dɩ chine a zie naanyʋŋʋlʋŋ.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Die ʋ hagɩ bʋnyɩ a dɩdɩ chʋŋ chʋŋ. Naa die dɩ yine wo, die dɩ paalɩ a yi be mamachi pam.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Ama Yisa dɩ yɩ ba nʋaŋ ta paalɩ balɩ ba dɩ ba daa keŋ a balɩ a yɩ vuoŋ ta balɩ ba dɩ ba yaalɩ jaaŋ a yɩ bʋa wa aŋ ʋ dii.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Die ba ga a ga a tʋgɩ mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ a keŋ jʋalɩ Genezariti tɩŋgbaŋ ma, a bɔbɩ haarɩkʋ mi.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Ba yɩa Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa jɩlɩma jigiŋ mana mana mɩŋ ama ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ tɩŋ vuosi aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ ʋ nɩmballɩ aŋaŋ ʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ deŋ ka yɩa wa jɩlɩma.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Naaŋ Herodi die dɩ wʋnna die ʋ baarɩ dɩ, “Ʋ yiwo Jɔɔn vuodieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ. Die n yi ba gobi wo ʋ sikpeŋ ama ʋ yiŋŋi a keŋ wo ʋ mɩsɩ ma.”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Yisa tʋntʋntɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale die ʋ tʋnna wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a keŋ a balɩ wa wudiekemba mana ba yine aŋaŋ ba dagɩna.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daaŋ die ʋ ga juu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juoku me a piili a daga kpɩkpaakʋ die dɩ benne mi Ŋmɩŋ wɩa. Daadamba die dɩ wʋnna ʋ dagɩkʋ die dɩ yi ba mana mana mamachi, die ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, “Sɩa ʋ ye wɩaha gie mana? Bɩa yɩaŋ wʋnna Ŋmɩŋ dɩ nagɩ a yɩ wa? Halɩ ʋ tʋmma mamachi tʋʋma.

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Daa ʋ yine kampɩnta ka? Daa Meri bʋa wa wonde? Daa ʋ yine Jemisi aŋaŋ Josefu aŋaŋ Judasi aŋaŋ Simoni mɩɩ wa? Daa ʋ tamba benne giena wa?” Ta ba zeti wo wo.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Ta die a yɩ ba nʋarɩ gie dɩ, “Daa nɩ pɔgɩlɩ jaaŋ a gamma nɩ dɩasɩsɩ ntaala, daaŋgbanɩŋ nyɩɩna ma. Nɩ daa keŋ a pɔgɩlɩ nyindiike, nɩ daa keŋ a pɔgɩlɩ bʋlɔgɩŋ nɩ daa keŋ a suu ligire nɩ jafasɩ ma.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Ama nɩ keŋ a tʋgɩ jigidieke ba keŋ a ka tuo nɩ yaa ba ka wʋŋ nɩ wʋbalɩka nɩ nyɩŋ mi ta pɩsɩ nɩ naatala kɔlɩŋ mi. Aŋ naa yi wɩɩŋ a gbara ba.”

ref: Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!” So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
text: Jɔɔn vuodieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ die ŋaaŋ a bala Herodi daaŋ daaŋ mana dɩ, “Dɩ wo sieŋ aŋaŋ fʋ baaŋ nan faarɩ fʋ nɩmbʋa hɔgʋ.”

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die Herodiasi die dɩ jɩɩ sɩnyɩɩrɩŋ a yɩ Jɔɔn, ta die a yaala ʋ kʋʋ wa, ama Herodi wɩa die ʋ ka bɩagɩ, dama Herodi gbaŋ die ka saaga.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Herodi die chɩga Jɔɔn, dama die ʋ sɩba a baarɩ Jɔɔn die yiwo vuovɩɩnɩŋ ta dɩgɩntɩ die dɩ wo ʋ sʋŋ. Die wɩa die ʋ ka yaala wɩɩŋ yi wo. Aŋaŋ die ʋ ŋaana wʋŋ Jɔɔn wʋbalɩkaha a daama wa mana yɔrɩ, die ʋ ŋaaŋ yaala ʋ wʋmma ʋ wʋbalɩka.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Die wɩa die bʋa wa die dɩ nyɩŋ a ga pɩasɩ ʋ nuŋ dɩ, “N baarɩ n yaala bɩa?” Ʋ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, “Baarɩ dɩ yaala Jɔɔn dieke dɩ sɩnana vuosi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ.”

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Die wɩa die bʋa wa dɩ chɩgɩ a ga naaŋ wa jigiŋ lagɩ lagɩ a baarɩ dɩ, “N yaala fʋ gobi Jɔɔn dieke die dɩ sɩnana vuosisi Ŋmɩŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a diisi kpalɩ ma a yɩ mɩŋ lelele.”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Naa die dɩ yi naaŋ wa sʋŋ die dɩ paalɩ a chʋʋsɩ pam, ama die ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a zeti dama ʋ kaana kaatɩ diekemba ʋ chaamba nɩŋŋa wa wɩa.

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: A die a nagɩ ka a diisi kpalɩ ma a yallɩka a keŋ a yɩ havʋʋbike, havʋʋbike die dɩ nagɩ ka a yɩ ʋ nuŋ.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Die Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ wʋnna, die ba ga a nagɩ Jɔɔn nyɩŋgbaŋka a ga guu. Yisa die dɩ yɩna dembisi tuse anʋ wa nyindiike dene (Mat.

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Vuosi pam die kienene ta yiŋŋe, die wɩa die Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die wo saŋŋa aŋaŋ ba baaŋ nan dii nyindiike. Die wɩa die Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩnɩŋ nɩ vaa aŋ tɩ ga haagɩyeŋ dieke nɩ baaŋ nan voosi.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Die wɩa die ba gbaŋ gbaŋ die ba juu haarɩŋ a gara jigidieke vuoŋ die dɩ wone.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Die jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a balɩ yɩ wa dɩ, “Ŋmɩnnɩ wɔŋ a juu mɩŋ ta giena dɩaŋ dɩ yi jigidieke jadiikiŋ dɩ worine ba baaŋ daa a dii.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩnɩŋ nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yaalɩ jaaŋ a yɩ ba aŋ ba dii.” Die ba pɩasɩ wa, yaala tɩ nagɩ sɩba dʋnɩa mana ligire a yaalɩ jaaŋ a yɩ ba aŋ ba dii?

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Paanʋsɩ aŋmɩa nɩ yallɩ? Nɩ ga a ga daansɩ a ye.” Die ba gana a ga daansɩ ta yiŋŋi a keŋ wo die ba balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, “Paanʋsɩ anʋ aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Yisa die dɩ balɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba yi vuosisi mana puo puo a lagɩsɩ a kala kala hɔɔyebiti me.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Dama die ba mana die dɩ yene wo ŋmaamɩŋ die yigine be. Yisa die dɩ a balɩ a yɩ ba bʋnyɩ dɩ, “Nɩ yigi sikimiŋ, wo manɩŋ nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yigi nɩ.”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ nagɩ paanʋsɩ anʋ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale wo, a daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Ʋ yi paanʋsɩsɩ a gbieri gbieri ta nagɩha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba tuohe a tɩa tɩa a yɩ vuosisi. Die ʋ bɩ nagɩ zaasɩtɩtɩ ale wo a gbieri gbieri a yɩ ba mana mana.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Ba mana mana die dɩ dii a chagɩ, ta tɩalɩ tɩalɩ.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ vaarɩ vaarɩ paanʋsɩ aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ tɩalɩkaha a suuli kparɩsɩ baŋ aŋaŋ ale.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Yisa die dɩ yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ juu haarɩkʋ lagɩ lagɩ a dɩ ʋ nɩŋŋa a gara mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ gaarɩ a ga Befisada, ta die ʋ wa a taanna vuosisi.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Die ʋ taanna vuosisi a kpatɩ wa die ʋ jʋalɩ ga kunkogiŋ me dɩ ʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Die jɩŋmɩŋ die dɩ tʋgɩna, haarɩkʋ die bie wo mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ sʋŋ aŋaŋ sʋŋ ta Yisa dɩ a ye bie tɩŋgbaŋ ma ʋ nyɩɩna.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Die ʋ ye ta ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die wahala aŋaŋ haarɩkʋ dɔmɩŋ, dama bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die nɩgɩnana ba a gara kʋaŋ kʋaŋ. Die wɩa die dɩ gana a tʋgɩ kparaasɩ kpaŋ sʋkʋleeliŋ, Yisa die dɩ chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ a kieŋ ba jigiŋ. Die ʋ chʋŋ a tɩanna ba ma

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: ama die ba ye wo ta ʋ chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeŋ. Die ba yene dɩ ʋ chʋŋ nyaabʋ sikpeku die ba keesi ta yi tama sɩba dɩ yiwo kogiŋ.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Womi die ʋ juu a gʋtɩ ba ma haarɩkʋ ma, bʋlɔgɩsɩbʋ die dɩ nan sɔmm. Die dɩ paalɩ a yi ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba mamachi pam

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: dama die ba ka sɩba die ʋ nagɩ paanʋsɩsɩ a yɩ vuosisi ba dii wo chɩaŋ. Ba sʋgɩtɩ ye ko bie wo lɩmɩŋ ma mɩŋ. Yisa die dɩ gbaanna yʋagɩtieliŋ dene (Mat.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Die ba nyɩnana haarɩkʋ ma vuosisi die dɩ mɩŋŋɩ Yisa bʋnyɩ.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die ba chɩgɩ ga juu tɩŋgbaŋka mana a chii yʋagɩtieliŋ a diisi ba kalɩŋasɩ sikpeŋ a ga jigidieke mana die ba wʋnna dɩ Yisa gaya wa.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Jigidieke mana die ʋ gana tɩŋkpaŋŋɩsɩ yaa tɩŋkpɩɩma vuosi ŋaaŋ chii wo ba yʋagɩtieliŋ a ga nyugiti ma ta jʋʋsa wa dɩ ʋ ko a vaa yʋagɩtielibe gbɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke kʋanʋaŋ, vuodiekemba mana mana die dɩ gbɩna ka die ye wo alaafɩa.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Tɔ, Farasisi aŋaŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ die dɩ nyɩnna Jerusalemi a keŋ die dɩ taaŋ giliŋ Yisa atɩaŋ.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Yisa die dɩ bɩ a balɩ a gʋtɩ ba dɩ “Nɩ yaa wa yɩaŋ sɩbɩŋ a zeti Ŋmɩŋ mɩra ta bɩagɩ a daga nɩ gbaŋ gbaŋ nɩ yaala wudiekemba.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Ba keŋ nyɩŋ nyugiti jige a keŋ, ba ka die sie ba nɩtɩ ba nuusi ta dɩɩsa ba chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩŋ wɩa, sɩba nyanyuule kpala daarɩŋ aŋaŋ gbiesi daarɩŋ aŋaŋ kalɩŋasɩ sugiriŋ wɩa.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Naa chɩaŋ wɩa die Farasisi aŋaŋ mɩra dɩdagɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ Yisa dɩ bɩa yine fʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ka dɩɩsa sie diekemba tɩ chɔɔŋkʋʋlɩba die dɩ nagɩna a yɩ tɩ wa ta die nyinti aŋaŋ nuudɩgɩnta.

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ ba dɩ, “Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die yaa wusie die ʋ bala nɩnɩŋ gɩgaantɩba gie wɩa sɩba ka maagɩna die wo dɩ: ‘Ŋmɩŋ baarɩ dɩ vuosi gie yɩa mɩŋ jɩlɩma aŋaŋ ba nʋa ama ba sʋgɩtɩ dɩ bie saasaa aŋaŋ mɩŋ.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: Dɩ yi wo yɔrɩ ba jɩama mɩŋ dama ba daga vuota ba gbaŋ gbaŋ ba mɩra sɩba a yiwo manɩŋ n mɩra.’ ”

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Nɩ zeti wo Ŋmɩŋ mɩra ta dɩɩsa vuotaŋ mɩra.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Ama nɩnɩŋ nɩ baarɩ dɩ ‘Vuoŋ dɩ keŋ a balɩ a yɩ ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ dɩ jadieke mana dɩ yine n sɩɩtɩ maŋ tɩŋ nan nagɩ a suŋŋi nɩ bɩrɩŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ jaaŋ.’

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Naa dɩ a keŋ a yi die nɩŋ nɩ chʋsa Ŋmɩŋ wʋbalɩkaha, nɩ dagɩnana vuosi die wo sʋŋ. Nɩ yie wɩa pam a nɩasa die.” Jadieke dɩ benne vuotaŋ sʋŋ ma wɩa (Mat.

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: Die ʋ ye a ko bala a gʋta dɩ “Wudieke dɩ nyɩnnana vuotaŋ sʋŋ yine ʋ yaa dɩgɩntɩ.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ bɩ a wa daadamba bɩbra a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ, ta balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩ mana mana chɩɩsɩma a wʋmma mɩŋ ta sɩmma n wʋbalɩkaha chɩasɩ.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: Die ʋ vana daadamba ta kuli tigiŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ pɩasɩ wa nandagɩrɩ chɩaŋ.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Yisa die dɩ pɩasɩ ba dɩ, “Nɩ gbaŋ nɩ ka sɩba ka chɩaŋ? Nɩ ka sɩba sɩba dɩ jadieke dɩ junene vuotaŋ nʋaŋ ma ka bɩagɩ yie wo ʋ yalla dɩgɩntɩ.

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: Dama a juu wo ʋ nyuuti ma a daaga nyɩŋgbaŋka a nyɩnna ama da ʋ sʋŋ ma.”

ref: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
text: aŋaŋ jome aŋaŋ kalinbaani aŋaŋ sugigoliŋ wuyiike aŋaŋ halɩ aŋaŋ gaanta aŋaŋ baga wori wuyiike.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Wʋbɩatɩ gie mana mana nyɩnna vuotaŋ sʋŋ a yie wo ʋ bɩrɩma vuodɩgɩntɩŋ.” Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ yada wɩa

ref: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
text: Hɔgʋ wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a baarɩ dɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, ka yiwo wusie, ama gbaasɩsɩ die dɩ dʋana teebulisi chɩaŋ gbaŋ naga ballɩbɩsɩ nyindiike chɔɔta die dɩ nannana.”

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Yisa die dɩ nyɩŋ mi a ga tɩŋ kaanɩ die dɩ bene Taya tɩŋgbaŋ ma, die ʋ ga a juu tigiŋ kaanɩ sʋŋ ta die a ka yaala vuoŋ ye wo, ama die ʋ ka bɩagɩ a lɔbɩrɩ ʋ gbaŋ.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Hɔgʋ wʋnyɩ die benne mi ta jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yallɩ ʋ havʋʋbiŋ, die ʋ wʋnna dɩ Yisa die bie mi die ʋ chɩgɩ a keŋ gbirigi Yisa nɩŋŋa.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: Hɔgʋ wa die ka yi Juu vuoŋ, die ba mɩɩrɩ wa wa Fonisia, Siria tɩŋgbaŋ ma ma. Die ʋ jʋʋsɩ Yisa dɩ ʋ yagɩ jɩmbɩatɩtɩ ʋ bʋa wa ma ma.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ama Yisa die yiŋŋi a balɩ wa dɩ, “Vaa tɩ woliŋ a yɩ ballɩbɩsɩ ba nyindiike, dɩ wo sieŋ aŋaŋ tɩ baaŋ nan nagɩ ballɩbɩsɩ nyindiike a yɩ gbaasɩ.”

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Die ʋ yiŋŋi a kuli ta ʋ bʋa wa dʋa gado sikpeŋ jɩmbɩatɩtɩ die dɩ seŋ a nyɩŋ ʋ ma. Yisa die dɩ gbaanna gaamʋ dene

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Yisa die dɩ wa a nyɩŋ Taya tɩŋgbaŋ ma a daagɩ Sadoni a chʋŋ a daagɩ tɩgɩsɩ baŋka sʋgɩtɩ a ga Galili mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma.

ref: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
text: Vuosi bataŋ die dɩ yaa daa wʋnyɩ die dɩ kana a ka mɩŋŋɩ bala wɩa a keŋ Yisa jigiŋ a jʋʋsɩ wa dɩ ʋ nagɩ ʋ nuusi a diisi ʋ ma.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Yisa die dɩ yigi daa wa nuuŋ me a yaa wa nyɩŋ lʋgɩŋ ta vaa daadamba. Yisa die dɩ nagɩ ʋ nuubisi a yi daa wa tɩba ma, ta tɩɩrɩ nɩntɔbɩtɩ ta gbɩ daa wa jiliŋ.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta paalɩ a voosi ta a balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ, “Effata!” Ka chɩaŋ wone, “Yuori!”

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Daa wa tɩbaha sʋŋ die dɩ yuori bʋnyɩ ʋ wʋmma wʋbalɩka ta bɩagɩ a bala wɩa vɩɩnɩŋ.

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ yɩ ba mana mana nʋaŋ dɩ ba sɩmma ba keŋ a balɩ wɩɩrɩ mi a yɩ vuoŋ. Ama saŋŋa dieke mana die Yisa dɩ a keŋ a balɩ die, die dɩ wana a faasɩ a yie ba bala wɩɩrɩ jigiŋ mana.

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Yisa die dɩ yɩ ba nʋaŋ a baarɩ, dɩ ba sɩmma Farasisi aŋaŋ Herodi vuosi wɩa.

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: Die ʋ paalɩ balɩ ba mɩŋ, die ʋ ka lɔbɩrɩ ba. Die Piita die dɩ yaa wa ga lʋgɩŋ a ga galɩŋ wa.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Zɔɔlɩŋ yigi mɩŋ mɩŋ pam aŋaŋ vuosi gie dama manɩŋ aŋaŋ ba daraa ataa wʋnna, ba ka bɩ yaa jaaŋ aŋaŋ ba baaŋ dii.
